MB_BUG_REPORT.insight: Master Bot (and other casual AI bots) can run out of time during their turn and then continue taking their turn indefinitely, causing the game to display a 'lost contact with the prismata AI thread' error. This was reported as occurring in both casual play and Master Bot games.
MB_BUG_REPORT.author: masn6811
MB_BUG_REPORT.context: masn6811 reported a recurring issue where the AI would run out of time and still keep taking its turn, with the error message persisting even after page refreshes.

MB_BUG_REPORT.insight: In the campaign puzzle 'Cryo Tactics II', a solution involving getting 4 Cryo Rays and freezing the Xeno unit only works due to bot misbehavior: the bot soaks on a Borehole Patroller instead of both shield barriers, meaning this solution would fail if the bot played correctly. The game's ending tip implies this bot-dependent solution is the intended one.
MB_BUG_REPORT.author: p0lari
MB_BUG_REPORT.context: apooche raised the issue that 'Cryo Tactics II' has two solutions, one of which only works due to bot misbehavior, and that the ending tip misleadingly implies the bot-dependent solution is intended.

MB_BUG_REPORT.insight: When using the absorb command (Q absorb), the bot soaks damage onto Xae rather than absorbing on Chieftain, because Chieftain has more HP and does not fit the standard soak heuristic. Elyot confirmed the heuristics do not handle Chieftain correctly and added it to the bug list.
MB_BUG_REPORT.author: silentslayers
MB_BUG_REPORT.context: silentslayers reported the absorb/soak misbehavior on Chieftain twice, and elyot confirmed it is a known bug due to heuristic limitations.

MB_BUG_REPORT.insight: A game freeze was reported by awaclus, possibly caused by The Wincer unit with a skin that had not been removed. The freeze was reproducible and a replay code was provided.
MB_BUG_REPORT.author: awaclus
MB_BUG_REPORT.context: awaclus reported a game freeze and provided a replay code, speculating that The Wincer with an unremoved skin may have caused it.

MB_BUG_REPORT.insight: Multiple game freezes were reported by masn6811 on 2020-12-04, attributed to unit skins (specifically an Arka skin and a Grimbotch skin). The freezes persisted through browser refresh and restart until a patch fix was deployed by elyot.
MB_BUG_REPORT.author: masn6811
MB_BUG_REPORT.context: masn6811 reported repeated game crashes tied to specific unit skins during active playtesting of the new patch, confirmed fixed by elyot after restarting the game.

MB_BUG_REPORT.insight: A separate game freeze was reported by masn6811 specifically caused by Chrono Filter, which persisted through refreshing the webpage, restarting the browser, and Ctrl+F5. Elyot confirmed it was a distinct bug from the skin-related freezes.
MB_BUG_REPORT.author: masn6811
MB_BUG_REPORT.context: masn6811 reported a Chrono Filter-related freeze that was unresolvable by the player, confirmed as a separate bug by developer elyot.

MB_BUG_REPORT.insight: The Q bot (used in events) incorrectly prefers to lose Engineers over 0-stamina Felids when choosing which units to sacrifice, suggesting a bug in the bot's unit-loss prioritization logic for event-specific units.
MB_BUG_REPORT.author: spiritfryer
MB_BUG_REPORT.context: Bug report about Q bot's incorrect sacrifice decisions in event mode, with the caveat that Q makes many errors in events due to incomplete unit-specific functionality.

MB_BUG_REPORT.insight: Master Bot appears to systematically ignore Cauterizer clicks and underdefends by 2 every time in a specific replay, even sacrificing an Arka unit as a consequence of this behavior.
MB_BUG_REPORT.author: liadahlia
MB_BUG_REPORT.context: liadahlia shared a replay link and noted that Master Bot repeatedly ignores Cauterizer clicks, underdefending by 2 each time and sacrificing an Arka unit.

MB_BUG_REPORT.insight: Master Bot resigned a game it was winning because it failed to account for Cluster Bolt, suggesting the bot does not correctly evaluate or anticipate certain click-ability interactions that can swing the game state.
MB_BUG_REPORT.author: amalloy
MB_BUG_REPORT.context: amalloy briefly reported this during the Dynamo absorb discussion thread.

MB_BUG_REPORT.insight: In a casual match (replay VNw+z-CskPt), a bot purchased all 10 available Mobile Animuses by turn 9 while spending only 2 red resources total across those turns, indicating a severe unit-purchase limiting failure in the bot's configuration for that unit.
MB_BUG_REPORT.author: amalloy
MB_BUG_REPORT.context: amalloy reported extreme bot misbehavior in a casual match, and elyot (developer) confirmed it could be fixed by adding a purchase limit config for that unit.

MB_BUG_REPORT.insight: After a code change intended to stop the bot from buying too many mobile Animuses, the bot may instead be buying and immediately clicking them, suggesting the fix introduced or exposed a new behavioral bug. A replay code was provided: qivv4-6CAIG.
MB_BUG_REPORT.author: vanitascabal6962
MB_BUG_REPORT.context: VanitasCabal asked Elyot whether the bot fix for over-purchasing mobile Animuses resulted in the bot immediately clicking them instead.

MB_BUG_REPORT.insight: Master Bot in a base-set-only game made the move WDD float 4GGG on turn 8, which is considered extremely poor play (buying a Wall then floating 4 gold with no productive use), indicating MB has no understanding of BSO opening theory.
MB_BUG_REPORT.author: extratricky
MB_BUG_REPORT.context: extratricky tested a BSO game against Master Bot to observe its behavior after community claimed MB doesn't know BSO lines.

MB_BUG_REPORT.insight: Master Bot caused a disconnect/crash by stalling long enough that Prismata announced it lost connection to the AI thread, forcing the human player to resign; a fix was reportedly being worked on by the developer.
MB_BUG_REPORT.author: amalloy
MB_BUG_REPORT.context: amalloy reported that Master Bot's AI thread connection dropped mid-game and waiting for reconnection did not help in his case.

MB_BUG_REPORT.insight: A bot (MoMoney) bought 9 Mobile Animuses, clicked them all, and spent only 2 red total across the entire game. Elyot investigated and found that a Mobile Animus buy limit added to the default limits was being overridden or ignored, likely due to details in Dave's code that Elyot did not fully understand.
MB_BUG_REPORT.author: amalloy
MB_BUG_REPORT.context: amalloy reported the bot massively over-buying Mobile Animuses and Elyot confirmed the buy limit was not working as intended.

MB_BUG_REPORT.insight: A bot (DrivenUpTheWall) passed its turn while floating 21 gold, which is a strict misplay since the most expensive unit costs 20 gold. The bot had built all available Drones, Engineers, and several Militias, then stopped purchasing anything after running out of Walls.
MB_BUG_REPORT.author: awaclus
MB_BUG_REPORT.context: awaclus observed a bot floating 21 gold and doing nothing, noting it as an obvious misplay given unit costs.

MB_BUG_REPORT.insight: In a game with Steelsplitters and Apollo, the intended sequence of clicking two Steelsplitters to attack a Gauss Cannon, having Apollo snipe the damaged Gauss Cannon, then clicking remaining Steelsplitters to attack the second Gauss Cannon does not work — clicking the remaining Steelsplitter cancels the Apollo action. This was replicated by community members and confirmed as a consistent behavior, though whether it is a bug or intended design is disputed.
MB_BUG_REPORT.author: itsotter
MB_BUG_REPORT.context: itsotter encountered this in a bot game and raised it; extratricky stated it has always worked this way, while Greyman planned to ask elyot for clarification.

MB_BUG_REPORT.insight: Master Bot buys Vivid Drone after Savior comes online, apparently because the drone supply is exhausted and it still wants to increase economy, but it fails to recognize that Vivid Drone actually reduces net gold income (costs 2 gold and 2 energy to produce -1 gold per turn net).
MB_BUG_REPORT.author: _theavatar
MB_BUG_REPORT.context: Specific observed instance of Master Bot buying Vivid Drones immediately after Savior came online, with community discussion of why this is wrong.

MB_BUG_REPORT.insight: Master Bot's Q auto-breach does not correctly account for fragile units that are not at their default health, causing it to, for example, put breach damage on a Gauss Cannon instead of killing four 1-health Colossi when breaching for exactly 4 damage. This has been a persistent bug.
MB_BUG_REPORT.author: apooche
MB_BUG_REPORT.context: amalloy noted Q auto-breach kills Tarsiers before 1-hp Ferals; apooche and sorawotobu confirmed the general bug that Q breach mishandles damaged fragile units.

MB_BUG_REPORT.insight: After a localization/patch update in September 2018, Master Bot began behaving like Wacky Bot in many games, reportedly due to an issue with localized text in card data. Players observed this happening in roughly half their games, with the bot making nonsensical decisions such as buying Barriers and Plexo Cells before any attack pressure existed.
MB_BUG_REPORT.author: elyot
MB_BUG_REPORT.context: After a patch that introduced localization, multiple players reported Master Bot playing randomly; developer elyot confirmed the bot was likely having an issue with localized card text.

MB_BUG_REPORT.insight: In the same September 2018 localization patch period, Guardian Rube (a bot opponent) was observed buying only 2 Gauss Cannons per turn even when it had resources to buy significantly more, indicating broken decision-making rather than standard Wacky Bot behavior.
MB_BUG_REPORT.author: shadourow
MB_BUG_REPORT.context: shadourow reported unusual bot behavior during a Guardian Rube game following the patch, describing it as 'definitely drunk rube' rather than normal Wacky Bot play.

MB_BUG_REPORT.insight: Clicking 'Claim Victory' during a bot game caused an automatic disconnect, occurring reproducibly at least twice in a row for one player following the September 2018 patch.
MB_BUG_REPORT.author: shadourow
MB_BUG_REPORT.context: shadourow reported being disconnected twice in a row when clicking 'claim victory' against a bot, suggesting a post-patch bug with bot game endings.

MB_BUG_REPORT.insight: Amalloy encountered a bug in the browser version where Master Bot built a Rhino that was invisible until the player reset to the beginning of the turn; the Rhino's damage (manifested as 2 Tarsiers dealing 3 damage) was the only indication it existed.
MB_BUG_REPORT.author: amalloy
MB_BUG_REPORT.context: Amalloy reported an unusual rendering/display bug while playing the browser version against Master Bot.

MB_BUG_REPORT.insight: A bug existed where playing with a gold handicap caused wins to add to a player's win streak but losses did not reset the streak. The bug was caused by code checking if the handicap-benefitting player was P1 rather than checking if they were the bot. This was reported by apooche and fixed by Elyot.
MB_BUG_REPORT.author: apooche
MB_BUG_REPORT.context: apooche reported a handicap win-streak bug; elyot confirmed it was a code error and fixed it.

MB_BUG_REPORT.insight: A bugged replay (code: bcmmA-YbKcj) shows Master Bot purchasing a Savior on turn 1, which is an anomalous opening. The replay also appears to show units from outside the active set, though this was later clarified to be a different-sets game option.
MB_BUG_REPORT.author: velizar_
MB_BUG_REPORT.context: velizar_ shared a replay flagged as bugged, noting Master Bot bought a Savior on turn 1 as an unusual behavior.

MB_BUG_REPORT.insight: The AI (bot) exhibits a seemingly inexplicable drone hold that achieves nothing strategically. Unlike other suboptimal plays (targeting frontline too much, always attacking with Rhinos) which stem from lack of effort, this drone hold appears to result from the AI actively putting in effort to reach a useless outcome.
MB_BUG_REPORT.author: drevoed
MB_BUG_REPORT.context: drevoed was analyzing an AI game and noted that while most bot mistakes are predictable and understandable, the drone hold was uniquely baffling as it appeared to achieve nothing.

MB_BUG_REPORT.insight: Attempting to buy 625 Gauss Charges in a single click caused the pacifist bot to crash and start attacking, likely due to an O(n^2) operation in the game logic triggered by a very large number of unit purchases in one action.
MB_BUG_REPORT.author: velizar_
MB_BUG_REPORT.context: velizar_ was attempting to set a maximum single-turn damage record and caused a game crash by buying an extreme number of Gauss Charges at once.

MB_BUG_REPORT.insight: Master Bot sometimes defends and then buys a Cluster Bolt on the same turn, creating a situation where the opponent can achieve an exact breach on a Centurion — a clear tactical error by the bot.
MB_BUG_REPORT.author: masn6811
MB_BUG_REPORT.context: Casual observation about a specific Master Bot blunder involving defense and Cluster Bolt purchase sequencing.

MB_BUG_REPORT.insight: Master Bot absorbs on an empty Bombarder and soaks on Matrix, indicating it does not correctly evaluate which units to assign defensive actions to when absorb/soak targets are suboptimal.
MB_BUG_REPORT.author: awaclus
MB_BUG_REPORT.context: awaclus shared a replay demonstrating the bot misassigning absorb and soak actions.

MB_BUG_REPORT.insight: Master Bot does not correctly account for Rhino's stamina when making decisions, leading to suboptimal play involving Rhinos. A similar issue manifests in the bot's handling of Bombarder absorb assignments.
MB_BUG_REPORT.author: velizar_
MB_BUG_REPORT.context: velizar_ noted the bot's Rhino stamina issue as a known bug mentioned previously in chat, in the context of discussing the Bombarder absorb bug replay.

MB_BUG_REPORT.insight: Master Bot buys Gauss Cannon charges even when they are certain to be absorbed by the opponent, indicating it does not correctly evaluate whether attack purchases will be wasted against the opponent's absorb.
MB_BUG_REPORT.author: velizar_
MB_BUG_REPORT.context: velizar_ observed the bot buying Gauss charges in a high-economy game where they would definitely be absorbed.

MB_BUG_REPORT.insight: Master Bot made seemingly random Apollo snipes that were not consistent with any identifiable strategic pattern, including sniping a Tarsier when the player had Steelsplitters available as a higher-priority target.
MB_BUG_REPORT.author: velizar_
MB_BUG_REPORT.context: velizar_ played a game where the bot acquired Apollo and its snipe choices appeared random and suboptimal.

MB_BUG_REPORT.insight: Master Bot buys Gauss Charges that deal zero damage because the opponent already has absorb (e.g. Urban Sentry or Infusion Grid) in play; it fails to recognize that the charges will be fully absorbed even when the absorbing unit is already constructed.
MB_BUG_REPORT.author: velizar_
MB_BUG_REPORT.context: velizar_ reported that Master Bot bought 2 Gauss Charges against an already-built Urban Sentry and also bought Gauss Charges that did nothing against an Infusion Grid; community discussed that the bot may not be looking ahead far enough to see the absorb.

MB_BUG_REPORT.insight: Master Bot made multiple severely suboptimal plays in a single game on replay 3@0pW-fHoKx, including not buying any defense despite having Valkyrion and Apollo available.
MB_BUG_REPORT.author: velizar_
MB_BUG_REPORT.context: velizar_ shared a replay and noted that not buying any defense when holding Valkyrion and Apollo is 'totally insane', indicating multiple severe bot errors in one game.

MB_BUG_REPORT.insight: Master Bot appears to not correctly account for Vai Mauronax's ability to pay attack to freeze a unit, leading it to overbuild defense by 1 more than necessary (blocking 4 above opponent's attack when only 3 above was required).
MB_BUG_REPORT.author: flakmaniak
MB_BUG_REPORT.context: Flakmaniak observed MB consistently over-blocking in a set that included Vai Mauronax, noting it wasn't a granularity issue since Husk was present and MB was buying Rhinos.

MB_BUG_REPORT.insight: Master Bot has been observed resigning in games where the player's only attackers were Clickers (very low damage units), suggesting the bot's resignation logic misevaluates board states. A specific replay demonstrates this behavior.
MB_BUG_REPORT.author: velizar_
MB_BUG_REPORT.context: Discussion about how the bot decides when to resign, prompted by velizar_ sharing a replay where MB resigned despite the opponent having minimal attacking capability.

MB_BUG_REPORT.insight: According to the PrismataAI source code, Master Bot's resignation logic triggers when the enemy's score is at least 1.3 times the bot's score (despite an outdated comment saying 1.5 times), where 'score' appears to represent total net worth of units currently on the board. This threshold-based resignation can cause the bot to resign or play on incorrectly in edge cases.
MB_BUG_REPORT.author: amalloy
MB_BUG_REPORT.context: amalloy linked to the PrismataAI open-source repository to clarify how the bot determines when to resign, following community discussion about bot resignation behavior.

MB_BUG_REPORT.insight: Master Bot misuses Cryo Ray chill ability: on turn 11 of the referenced replay, it wastes 6 Cryo Ray shots chilling Walls that do not affect the outcome of the block, when chilling the Barrier instead would have been correct.
MB_BUG_REPORT.author: alex319
MB_BUG_REPORT.context: alex319 reported a specific reproducible case of Master Bot wasting chill shots on irrelevant Wall units instead of the Barrier.

MB_BUG_REPORT.insight: Master Bot uses Plexo Cell instead of absorbing damage, representing a specific suboptimal decision in resource/defense management.
MB_BUG_REPORT.author: velizar_
MB_BUG_REPORT.context: velizar_ shared a replay explicitly noting the bot uses Plexo Cell instead of absorb.

MB_BUG_REPORT.insight: In replay m+Yhp-rSaTr, Master Bot chose to kill two Wild Drones rather than breach the opponent for Lucina plus 1 Tarsier, missing what appears to be the stronger play.
MB_BUG_REPORT.author: velizar_
MB_BUG_REPORT.context: velizar_ shared a replay where Master Bot made a suboptimal attacking decision, choosing to kill Wild Drones instead of breaching for higher-value targets.

MB_BUG_REPORT.insight: Master Bot was observed playing like Wacky Bot (e.g., buying Engineers turn 1 into Animus), suggesting a possible bot identity mixup or degraded AI behavior following server instability.
MB_BUG_REPORT.author: ruinedshadows
MB_BUG_REPORT.context: ruinedshadows reported Master Bot behaving like Wacky Bot shortly after server hiccups in April 2023.

MB_BUG_REPORT.insight: A client bug caused a player clicking to play versus Master Bot to receive a 'game failed to load' error with Master Bot declared winner, while a live game was actually created and visible in the live games list. The player had to manually restart the client and rejoin the game.
MB_BUG_REPORT.author: steel0229e
MB_BUG_REPORT.context: steel0229e reported a reproducible client/server bug when launching a Master Bot game during the April 2023 server instability period.

MB_BUG_REPORT.insight: Q defense incorrectly prefers to soak Perforators over Steelsplitters in certain situations, likely because it does not recognize Perforators as permanent attackers and treats them as lower-priority soak targets compared to Steelsplitters.
MB_BUG_REPORT.author: sano712
MB_BUG_REPORT.context: Sano observed Q choosing to soak 3 Perforators over 2 Steelsplitters and questioned the algorithm's logic.

MB_BUG_REPORT.insight: Master Bot (3s) will buy Tia Threnody and attempt to 'absorb' on it while simultaneously soaking on Centurion, demonstrating a misunderstanding of which units are appropriate absorb/soak targets.
MB_BUG_REPORT.author: .holyfire
MB_BUG_REPORT.context: .holyfire reported a specific replay showing MB misusing Tia and Centurion for absorb/soak in an incorrect way.

MB_BUG_REPORT.insight: Master Bot crashes and falls back to random moves under certain conditions. The most reliable known trigger is playing a large number of units when there is no unit limit. Other crash triggers exist but are not remembered by the reporter.
MB_BUG_REPORT.author: velizar_
MB_BUG_REPORT.context: velizar_ responded to spiritfryer's vague recollection about the bot 'going stupid sometimes', confirming a crash-to-random-moves bug.

MB_BUG_REPORT.insight: Master Bot exhibits 'Galvanophobia': it targets Galvani Drone with breach damage instead of strictly superior targets such as Drones, or even instead of breaching at all (e.g., killing two Galvani Drones instead of a Wall and a Drone).
MB_BUG_REPORT.author: .holyfire
MB_BUG_REPORT.context: .holyfire reporting MB prioritizing Galvani Drone kills over objectively better breach targets.

MB_BUG_REPORT.insight: Master Bot will click Asteri even when there is no possibility that the barrier ability will have any effect on the game state.
MB_BUG_REPORT.author: .holyfire
MB_BUG_REPORT.context: .holyfire noting an observed MB behavior of uselessly activating Asteri.

MB_BUG_REPORT.insight: Master Bot will absorb on a Borehole Patroller in preference to a Steelsplitter or Doomed Wall, because its valuation is cost-based rather than based on actual in-game defensive value.
MB_BUG_REPORT.author: liquideggproduct
MB_BUG_REPORT.context: liquideggproduct observing MB absorbing on Patroller over Splitter and Doomed Wall, with apooche confirming it is because Patroller costs more.

MB_BUG_REPORT.insight: Master Bot has a known architecture limitation: it uses approximately four fixed defense algorithms and applies MCTS only to choose among them. When all four algorithms are suboptimal for a given situation, MB cannot defend correctly.
MB_BUG_REPORT.author: apooche
MB_BUG_REPORT.context: apooche explaining MB's internal defense architecture after .holyfire noted cases where MB defends worse than Q-defense.

MB_BUG_REPORT.insight: Q-defense (the game's auto-defense algorithm) can be confused by specific combinations of defenders: with Wall, Forcefield, two Rhinos (all prompt) and a Steelsplitter plus Infusion Grid, Q-defense incorrectly soaks on the Steelsplitter instead of the Wall. Manually clicking a Rhino first and then pressing Q corrects the behavior.
MB_BUG_REPORT.author: .holyfire
MB_BUG_REPORT.context: .holyfire reporting an unusual Q-defense bug with a specific combination of blocking units.

MB_BUG_REPORT.insight: Master Bot resigned two turns before a player's Savior even finished construction, suggesting MB incorrectly evaluates or over-weights the threat of Savior before it becomes active.
MB_BUG_REPORT.author: .holyfire
MB_BUG_REPORT.context: .holyfire shared a game where MB resigned prematurely upon seeing a Savior purchase.

MB_BUG_REPORT.insight: Master Bot lost a game that would otherwise have been a draw because it had a charge remaining on its Venge Cannon, suggesting MB may mismanage Venge Cannon charges in close endgame situations.
MB_BUG_REPORT.author: .holyfire
MB_BUG_REPORT.context: .holyfire shared a frustrating game where MB's Venge Cannon charge prevented what should have been a draw.

MB_BUG_REPORT.insight: Master Bot was observed buying a second Blastforge in a set that contained neither Drake nor Grenade Mech, which was noted as illogical tech acquisition with no apparent purpose.
MB_BUG_REPORT.author: _theavatar
MB_BUG_REPORT.context: Player observed Master Bot making an apparently irrational tech purchase during a game.

MB_BUG_REPORT.insight: In the referenced replay, Master Bot sacrificed a Wall to save a Corpus and then clicked it, which masn6811 and velizar_ agreed was clearly incorrect play. The game was described as already lost by that point, with multiple winning lines available earlier on turn 11 involving Wall+Corpus or Steelsplitter+Husk+Drone.
MB_BUG_REPORT.author: masn6811
MB_BUG_REPORT.context: masn6811 posted a replay noting Master Bot could have won the endgame, and multiple experts identified the specific turn 11 misplay and alternative winning lines.

MB_BUG_REPORT.insight: There appears to be a bug where the 'But How?' achievement (lose to Pacifist Bot) does not trigger correctly in some circumstances. Multiple failed attempts were recorded; the player suspected the non-English game localization may have interfered, though mrguy888 noted a prior similar bug report existed. Replay codes for failed attempts and one successful attempt were provided.
MB_BUG_REPORT.author: cachotognax
MB_BUG_REPORT.context: cachotognax attempted to get the 'But How?' achievement against Pacifist Bot and failed multiple times before succeeding after switching to English language, suspecting a localization bug.

MB_COMPARISON.insight: The casual match bots (e.g., EpiCRusheR) are designed to execute specific strategies hard rather than play optimally, making them educational for players unfamiliar with those strategies but trivially easy for experienced players; Master Bot and BottyMcBotFace are the only casual bots considered genuinely difficult.
MB_COMPARISON.author: consensus
MB_COMPARISON.context: darkeisbein and bifurcatedbitch discussing the design intent and relative difficulty of casual match bots versus Master Bot.

MB_COMPARISON.insight: BottyMcBotFace is over 100 rating points stronger than Master Bot in casual match play. It uses Master Bot's engine but augments it with a large opening book of strong rushes, and employs alpha-beta pruning to decide between rush and normal Master Bot builds.
MB_COMPARISON.author: elyot
MB_COMPARISON.context: Developer elyot investigated casual match bot matchmaking quality after player complaints about being matched with weak bots.

MB_COMPARISON.insight: Among casual match bots, the strongest tier includes BottyMcBotFace and variants of Master Bot with adjusted opening books. Bots like 'deep_drone' and 'Masterbot2016' were considered the best of the remaining casual bots, with Masterbot2016 being roughly comparable to modern ExpertBot.
MB_COMPARISON.author: consensus
MB_COMPARISON.context: Community discussion about which casual match bots were strongest, following elyot's investigation into matchmaking quality.

MB_COMPARISON.insight: According to Steel, casual match bot matchmaking was completely random rather than skill-based, and all casual bots were weaker than Master Bot. deep_drone and Masterbot2016 were considered the best of the casual bots, as they at least made semi-reasonable openings.
MB_COMPARISON.author: steel0229e
MB_COMPARISON.context: Steel disagreed with elyot's claim that matchmaking would prioritize stronger bots for stronger players, stating the system was fully random.

MB_COMPARISON.insight: According to a 2015 academic paper on the Prismata AI, Master Bot played at approximately the top 25% of human players at the time of writing. However, velizar_ estimated that by 2020 it was likely only in the top 85% of current active players, suggesting the human playerbase has improved relative to the bot.
MB_COMPARISON.author: velizar_
MB_COMPARISON.context: Players discussing a cited statistic from the PrismataAI academic paper about bot strength relative to the human ladder.

MB_COMPARISON.insight: Rush-focused casual bots (e.g., bots designed around aggressive red strategies) can outperform Master Bot in specific low-economy red rush scenarios because they execute strategies that play to bot strengths, whereas Master Bot's broader strategic approach is weaker in those specific matchups.
MB_COMPARISON.author: namington
MB_COMPARISON.context: namington and masn6811 discussed how rush casual bots like HavoCRusheR can be better than MB in low-econ red sets.

MB_COMPARISON.insight: Master Bot uses alpha-beta search with heuristics that generate a small subset of moves to evaluate each turn, rather than any neural network or data-driven component. Community members note this architecture makes it meaningfully weaker than a hypothetical NN-based bot trained on game data.
MB_COMPARISON.author: mtanzer
MB_COMPARISON.context: Discussion about the feasibility of building a stronger Prismata AI to rival top players, with mtanzer describing Master Bot's technical architecture.

MB_COMPARISON.insight: When casual bots (Rush Bot or Breachproof Bot) receive a favorable unit pool in min-unit-count games, they can be harder to beat than Master Bot; Master Bot is better at tactics than strategy, making it more dangerous in smaller sets where strategy matters less.
MB_COMPARISON.author: p0lari
MB_COMPARISON.context: Discussion of how to get more challenging bot games by adjusting the unit count slider in casual queue.

MB_COMPARISON.insight: Wacky Bot is built directly into the Prismata client, while Master Bot, Adept Bot, and BottyMcBotFace run as a separate external executable (PrismataAI.exe). If the external AI crashes or times out, the client-side Wacky Bot automatically takes over. A client hang of several seconds before resuming can be a symptom of the external AI timing out rather than a true client crash.
MB_COMPARISON.author: apooche
MB_COMPARISON.context: Technical discussion about how the AI bots are implemented and what happens when they fail mid-game.

MB_COMPARISON.insight: Master Bot is significantly stronger than the other built-in bots (BottyMcBotFace, Wacky Bot, Adept Bot), and community consensus is that players should practice exclusively against Master Bot once past the beginner stage, as the others are too easy to provide meaningful competition.
MB_COMPARISON.author: consensus
MB_COMPARISON.context: Multiple community members advising a new player on how to progress, with f0x and others recommending Master Bot exclusively over other bots.

MB_COMPARISON.insight: BottyMcBotFace is essentially Master Bot (7-second think time) with an additional rush database, which makes it marginally stronger than standard Master Bot in rush-heavy situations, but not significantly stronger overall.
MB_COMPARISON.author: f300xen
MB_COMPARISON.context: Discussion about bot difficulty tiers; f0x clarified the technical difference between BottyMcBotFace and Master Bot 7s.

MB_COMPARISON.insight: Adept Bot handles rush strategies differently from Master Bot; a player reported being able to beat Master Bot consistently but losing to Adept Bot, suggesting Adept Bot may have stronger or more specialized anti-rush responses.
MB_COMPARISON.author: miuna6933
MB_COMPARISON.context: New player discussion about which bot to practice against; miuna6933 shared personal experience of Adept Bot being harder than expected against rush strategies.

MB_COMPARISON.insight: Wacky Bot plays completely randomly without any strategic logic and is not intended as a skill benchmark; it cannot even reliably purchase Drones, making it only useful for ego-boosting or entertainment rather than practice.
MB_COMPARISON.author: consensus
MB_COMPARISON.context: Multiple community members described Wacky Bot's behavior when a new player asked about practicing against bots; valgrim_the_demon noted Wacky Bot cannot drone, confirmed by supergreyman.

MB_COMPARISON.insight: The casual bots (BottyMcBotFace, Wacky Bot, Adept Bot) follow specific strategies and are described as slightly easier than Master Bot, but not by a large margin.
MB_COMPARISON.author: ruinedshadows
MB_COMPARISON.context: A new player asked about the difficulty difference between casual bots and Master Bot.

MB_COMPARISON.insight: Expert Bot is recommended as a meaningful stepping stone between the campaign/advanced exercises and Master Bot, as many players who beat Expert Bot consistently still lose regularly to Master Bot. Master Bot difficulty is significantly higher than Expert Bot.
MB_COMPARISON.author: consensus
MB_COMPARISON.context: Community discussion around a new player onboarding guide, with amalloy, supergreyman, and willflame all agreeing Expert Bot should precede Master Bot in the progression.

MB_COMPARISON.insight: Master Bot received an explicit override rule to stop buying too many Mobas (a unit type), but other bots such as Wacky Bot did not receive this correction, meaning lower-tier bots lack some of the hand-tuned decision guardrails that Master Bot has.
MB_COMPARISON.author: amalloy
MB_COMPARISON.context: amalloy noted that Lunarch had to add explicit rules to override normal bot decision-making for Master Bot specifically, distinguishing it from other bots.

MB_COMPARISON.insight: Community members considered Master Bot to be approximately Tier 6 in strength, strong enough that it takes new players many hours (sometimes 40+ hours of play) to beat it consistently, making it a meaningful skill benchmark before transitioning to ranked play.
MB_COMPARISON.author: ruinedshadows
MB_COMPARISON.context: ruinedshadows and zheliu discussing how long it takes new players to beat Master Bot consistently, with zheliu noting a forum post of a player with 40 hours still unable to do so.

MB_COMPARISON.insight: Master Bot is estimated at approximately 1100 Elo strength, making it beatable by players above roughly 1600 rating. Players who previously found it easy to beat when knowing it was Master Bot performed noticeably worse against it when it was disguised as a ladder opponent, suggesting exploitable patterns are a significant component of its apparent weakness.
MB_COMPARISON.author: mrguy888
MB_COMPARISON.context: mrguy888 estimating Master Bot's rating in context of discussing whether it could reach Tier 9 if it laddered today.

MB_COMPARISON.insight: If Master Bot crashes, the game reverts to Wacky Bot, which is the only bot entirely contained within the client. Master Bot relies on external components that can crash, while Wacky Bot is fully local.
MB_COMPARISON.author: mrguy888
MB_COMPARISON.context: mrguy888 explained the fallback behavior when Master Bot crashes during a game.

MB_COMPARISON.insight: Casual Match bots are strictly worse than Master Bot strategically. Awaclus dismisses them as inferior, implying Master Bot is the strongest available AI option in the game.
MB_COMPARISON.author: awaclus
MB_COMPARISON.context: Discussion of playing bots while queuing for ranked, with awaclus noting Casual Match bots are weaker than Master Bot.

MB_COMPARISON.insight: Master Bot is described as 'pretty competent by bot standards' and noticeably harder to beat than the other available bots. New players are advised to start against bots and can advance considerably in skill before consistently beating Master Bot.
MB_COMPARISON.author: consensus
MB_COMPARISON.context: Community members advising a new player (cat8472) on how to get started, comparing bot difficulty levels.

MB_COMPARISON.insight: Wacky Bot is considered an appropriate starting point for complete beginners before progressing to higher-level bots; at least one expert (bky) started with Wacky Bot as well. Expert Bot is an intermediate challenge, and Master Bot is the strongest built-in opponent requiring approximately one to two months of regular play to beat consistently.
MB_COMPARISON.author: consensus
MB_COMPARISON.context: Community members advised matrix30815 on a progression path through the available bots.

MB_COMPARISON.insight: Master Bot is described as a meaningful skill bridge for new players: players who can beat Master Bot consistently have a real chance against older human players, but top ladder players can beat Master Bot almost every game due to knowledge of its exploitable patterns.
MB_COMPARISON.author: consensus
MB_COMPARISON.context: Community discussion advising a new player on how bots compare to human opponents on ladder.

MB_COMPARISON.insight: Among the available bots, Deep Drone is rated higher than BottyMcBotFace. Both are described as Master Bot with a larger opening book, with Deep Drone specifically noted as having a higher rating in bot comparison spreadsheet data.
MB_COMPARISON.author: shadourow
MB_COMPARISON.context: A player asked if there was a better AI than Master Bot, leading to a discussion of bot ratings from a shared spreadsheet.

MB_COMPARISON.insight: BottyMcBotFace (and Deep Drone) are essentially Master Bot augmented with a larger opening book, making them stronger than raw Master Bot primarily in the opening phase of the game.
MB_COMPARISON.author: jamesa7171
MB_COMPARISON.context: Discussion about whether any bot is stronger than Master Bot; jamesa7171 and shadourow confirmed the opening-book distinction.

MB_COMPARISON.insight: Wacky Bot occasionally plays the optimal move by chance, meaning every player, including strong ones, can make plays worse than Wacky Bot at some point.
MB_COMPARISON.author: awaclus
MB_COMPARISON.context: Lighthearted exchange between velizar_ and awaclus about Wacky Bot's random nature occasionally producing optimal moves.

MB_COMPARISON.insight: The 7s Master Bot is not significantly stronger than the 3s Master Bot in practice; the main advantage of playing against the 7s bot is that it takes longer turns, giving the human player more total time to think each round (6s per round vs 3s, or 10s vs 7s combined).
MB_COMPARISON.author: awaclus
MB_COMPARISON.context: awaclus explained that playing the 7s bot is easier mainly because of extra thinking time for the human, not because the bot itself plays much better.

MB_COMPARISON.insight: Multiple bots (BottyMcBotFace, HitQ2MakeItRain, CaptainTryhard, HavoCRusheR) all display as Tier IV 86% in Giselle replay info despite having wildly different internal ratings, suggesting the displayed tier is a default placeholder rather than a reflection of actual bot strength.
MB_COMPARISON.author: jamesa7171
MB_COMPARISON.context: Community members noticing inconsistency between displayed tier ratings and known internal bot ratings when reviewing replays in Giselle.

MB_COMPARISON.insight: Master Bot was reportedly placed on the ranked ladder in an earlier era and achieved approximately Tier 6; however, this was a different version of Master Bot under a different rating system, making direct comparison to current rankings unreliable.
MB_COMPARISON.author: steel0229e
MB_COMPARISON.context: Discussion about how Master Bot would perform if placed on the modern ranked ladder.

MB_COMPARISON.insight: BottyMcBotFace has a larger opening book than Master Bot but otherwise uses the same underlying logic for the rest of the game.
MB_COMPARISON.author: shadourow
MB_COMPARISON.context: Conversation comparing bot strengths in the context of whether BottyMcBotFace is a 'casual bot'.

MB_COMPARISON.insight: Some players prefer Adept Bot over Master Bot for practice because Adept Bot plays with a higher-econ style, which can be useful for players who want to practice against specific economic strategies.
MB_COMPARISON.author: shadourow
MB_COMPARISON.context: shadourow mentioned that a player named Cooled plays Adept Bot rather than Master Bot specifically for higher econ games.

MB_COMPARISON.insight: Master Bot is described as appearing in casual matchmaking in disguise (indistinguishable from human or weaker bots to new players), identifiable mainly by longer computation time (~8 seconds per turn). A 'MasterBot 2016' version was mentioned as beatable by a new player, with community noting the AI had not significantly improved since then.
MB_COMPARISON.author: wontrice, supergreyman
MB_COMPARISON.context: New player discussing their record against bots and asking about the difference between casual bots and Master Bot.

MB_EXPLOIT_STRATEGY.insight: The achievement requiring the player to beat Master Bot while giving it a 5-gold handicap must be done with fully random units (honorable game requirement); players cannot pick a unit set specifically designed to exploit bot weaknesses. The achievement is considered very doable by playing efficiently.
MB_EXPLOIT_STRATEGY.author: iminabearsuit
MB_EXPLOIT_STRATEGY.context: awaclus asking whether the 5-gold handicap MB achievement could be gamed by choosing a set the bot is weak against; iminabearsuit confirmed random units are required.

MB_EXPLOIT_STRATEGY.insight: Against bots weaker than Master Bot, absorbing every turn without ever getting breached accounts for roughly 50% of the success needed to beat them, making efficient defense the primary skill to develop.
MB_EXPLOIT_STRATEGY.author: steel0229e
MB_EXPLOIT_STRATEGY.context: A newer player was struggling to beat Expert bot despite having a better economy; steel0229e and crash_overlord explained over-econing and breach avoidance as the key issues.

MB_EXPLOIT_STRATEGY.insight: Master Bot frequently executes a 'fake breachproof' strategy using Thorium Dynamo: it converts drones into Dynamos and builds Gauss Cannons, allowing the opponent to breach and deal 4+ damage to a Dynamo while simultaneously constructing Blastforges, then transitions to defense after absorbing significant damage for free. The correct counter is to avoid putting damage on the Dynamo and instead target Gauss Cannons or other units when Blastforges are under construction.
MB_EXPLOIT_STRATEGY.author: consensus
MB_EXPLOIT_STRATEGY.context: bky described losing 10+ gold value to Master Bot's Dynamo absorb trick, confirmed by mtanzer and masn6811 who also lost to it.

MB_EXPLOIT_STRATEGY.insight: In Arms Race sets, Master Bot predictably buys multiple Vivid Drones in consecutive turns, making its behavior in those sets highly readable and exploitable by opponents who can plan around this pattern.
MB_EXPLOIT_STRATEGY.author: amalloy
MB_EXPLOIT_STRATEGY.context: amalloy casually noted the bot's recognizable Arms Race behavior as a way to identify the set type from bot actions alone.

MB_EXPLOIT_STRATEGY.insight: A turn-1 Animus into rush (e.g. Smorcus) against Master Bot is an effective strategy for earning the Rush Master achievement because Master Bot misresponds with inefficient tech purchases rather than proper defensive or counter-rush lines.
MB_EXPLOIT_STRATEGY.author: vanitascabal6962
MB_EXPLOIT_STRATEGY.context: Player described using turn-1 Animus into rush unit against Master Bot to easily obtain the Rush Master achievement, with mrguy888 confirming Master Bot is known to be bad at Prismata.

MB_EXPLOIT_STRATEGY.insight: In the 'Totally Unfair' challenge, multiple strategies have been reported to work against Master Bot: a high-economy build (buying 27 drones plus strong soak units to extend the game and let the bot make mistakes), Feral Wardens, and Blood Pact. Feral Wardens was confirmed as a successful approach by multiple players, while Blood Pact was harder to execute.
MB_EXPLOIT_STRATEGY.author: consensus
MB_EXPLOIT_STRATEGY.context: Players discussed viable strategies for beating Master Bot in the Totally Unfair challenge, with sorawotobu and awaclus sharing their successful approaches.

MB_EXPLOIT_STRATEGY.insight: Being able to beat Master Bot consistently is treated as the community benchmark for readiness to enter ranked play, implying that players who can reliably beat Master Bot have developed sufficient strategic fundamentals to compete at the lower end of the ranked ladder.
MB_EXPLOIT_STRATEGY.author: consensus
MB_EXPLOIT_STRATEGY.context: Multiple experienced players (awaclus, darkeisbein, vanitascabal6962) discussed the progression path from bots to ranked play.

MB_EXPLOIT_STRATEGY.insight: Using the in-game chat command /RotatingInferno converts Master Bot into Wacky Bot behavior, making it trivially easy to beat with certain unit combinations such as buying out the Animus and using a Fire Spinner (Scorchilla) while the bot does nothing effective.
MB_EXPLOIT_STRATEGY.author: p0lari
MB_EXPLOIT_STRATEGY.context: Discussion about setting up favorable unit sets to beat the bot for dailies or challenges; p0lari discovered /RotatingInferno switches Master Bot to Wacky Bot behavior.

MB_EXPLOIT_STRATEGY.insight: Including Venge Cannon in the set makes it trivially easy to beat Master Bot; the bot cannot handle the Venge Cannon all-in strategy and will reliably lose to it.
MB_EXPLOIT_STRATEGY.author: f300xen
MB_EXPLOIT_STRATEGY.context: f300xen and awaclus discussed strategies for beating Master Bot to earn daily rewards, with awaclus confirming that including Venge Cannon in the set essentially guarantees a win.

MB_EXPLOIT_STRATEGY.insight: Master Bot can be cheesed to win every game with zero skill, making it viable for fast experience grinding via bullet games.
MB_EXPLOIT_STRATEGY.author: mrguy888
MB_EXPLOIT_STRATEGY.context: Discussion about fastest ways to level up in Prismata, with mrguy888 recommending bullet vs Master Bot as exploitable for guaranteed wins.

MB_EXPLOIT_STRATEGY.insight: A Steelsplitter rush is a reliable strategy to beat Wacky Bot, implying Wacky Bot cannot adequately defend against early aggressive Steelsplitter pressure.
MB_EXPLOIT_STRATEGY.author: mrguy888
MB_EXPLOIT_STRATEGY.context: Brief mention during a general social conversation thread about streamers and the game.

MB_EXPLOIT_STRATEGY.insight: Knowing Master Bot's predictable weaknesses (absorb denial response, overkill attack behavior, no gambits, no tech purchases) allows players to make plays that are suboptimal against strong humans but optimal against the bot. This knowledge gap means players who know they are facing Master Bot have a measurably higher winrate than against an anonymous opponent of equivalent strength.
MB_EXPLOIT_STRATEGY.author: amalloy
MB_EXPLOIT_STRATEGY.context: Discussion of whether Master Bot was harder to beat when disguised as a ladder opponent; amalloy argues exploitable patterns require knowing you face the bot.

MB_EXPLOIT_STRATEGY.insight: The Extremely Unfair Challenge bot can be beaten using only Feral as the advanced set unit, with no Blastforge required. The recommended line is DD / DD / DDA / TTC / then Engineer+Rhino+Feral each turn from turns 5-15, fully utilizing Rhino and Feral for attack and defense.
MB_EXPLOIT_STRATEGY.author: consensus
MB_EXPLOIT_STRATEGY.context: Multiple players (sano712, zera777, apooche) converged on this line as the optimal way to beat the Extremely Unfair Challenge, with apooche confirming Feral is the only advanced unit required.

MB_EXPLOIT_STRATEGY.insight: In Tarsimus (base set restricted to Animus and Tarsier with starting units, no Drones or Engineers), a human P2 can reliably beat Master Bot using a fastimus opening, exploiting the severe first-player advantage that exists in this restricted unit set.
MB_EXPLOIT_STRATEGY.author: amalloy
MB_EXPLOIT_STRATEGY.context: Discussion of whether Master Bot's performance in constrained sets proves game-theoretic outcomes; amalloy notes MB can lose these setups.

MB_EXPLOIT_STRATEGY.insight: Including Zemora Voidbringer and Mobile Animus in a set is a reliable way to exploit Master Bot, as MB cannot handle these units effectively and will likely lose. This was used to farm Master Bot achievements.
MB_EXPLOIT_STRATEGY.author: nvlx
MB_EXPLOIT_STRATEGY.context: nvlx described rigging all Master Bot achievements by including Zemora and Mobile Animus in the set, with shadourow confirming that all-defense sets also work.

MB_EXPLOIT_STRATEGY.insight: Master Bot is competent with small red units like Grimbotch and Vore, defaulting to a fast Animus build. However, it cannot defend against aggressive Smorcus rushes (including Elyot Animus Smorcus rush or Crazimus Smorcus rush), making Smorcus rush a reliable exploit strategy against it.
MB_EXPLOIT_STRATEGY.author: masn6811
MB_EXPLOIT_STRATEGY.context: masn6811 identifying specific MB strengths and weaknesses with red units, confirming Smorcus rush as a viable exploit.

MB_EXPLOIT_STRATEGY.insight: Offering gambit attacks that require accepting a Rhino-onto-Wall or Wall-onto-Rhino sacrifice is a reliable exploit against Master Bot because it will always keep its defensive units rather than accept such gambits, even when accepting would enable a breach of the opponent's key infrastructure like Drone Grid.
MB_EXPLOIT_STRATEGY.author: masn6811
MB_EXPLOIT_STRATEGY.context: masn6811 explaining how the bot's Q-defense-only model can be exploited by human gambit play.

MB_EXPLOIT_STRATEGY.insight: Wacky Bot plays randomly and offers no learning value; even weak bots are better opponents for improving. Shadourow and deadhour both recommend new players avoid Wacky Bot entirely and start with Basic Bot instead.
MB_EXPLOIT_STRATEGY.author: consensus
MB_EXPLOIT_STRATEGY.context: Community advising a new player struggling to win against random bots, explaining Wacky Bot is random and not useful for improvement.

MB_EXPLOIT_STRATEGY.insight: A reliable low-effort strategy to beat Master Bot is buying Rectifiers on 11 Drones and 2 Conduits, which Zera reported giving a 26-game win streak against Master Bot.
MB_EXPLOIT_STRATEGY.author: zera777
MB_EXPLOIT_STRATEGY.context: Zera described their best Master Bot win streak and the 'cheesy, low-effort' strategy used to achieve it.

MB_EXPLOIT_STRATEGY.insight: A reliable strategy to beat Master Bot involves a Galvani Drone + Venge Cannon + Cluster Bolt set where P1 opens 11C, CC, CC, double Venge Cannon into Cluster Bolts. Lyra describes this as the easiest known way to beat the bot.
MB_EXPLOIT_STRATEGY.author: lyra1712
MB_EXPLOIT_STRATEGY.context: Discussion of multiple strategies known to exploit Master Bot weaknesses, prompted by shadourow's Kappa challenge.

MB_EXPLOIT_STRATEGY.insight: A highly reliable strategy to beat Master Bot uses Galvani Drone plus Venge Cannon plus Cluster Bolt. The suggested P1 line is C11, CC, CC, 22G into Cluster Bolts. Multiple community members confirmed Galvani Drone as a consistent pattern across easy Master Bot sets.
MB_EXPLOIT_STRATEGY.author: lyra1712
MB_EXPLOIT_STRATEGY.context: Community discussion seeking the easiest honorable set to beat Master Bot, converging on Galvani Drone as a recurring element in the best strategies.

MB_EXPLOIT_STRATEGY.insight: When farming Master Bot win count (rather than a streak), a Tia Threnody rush subset is advantageous because losses occur quickly, allowing fast retries. For streaks, sets exploiting Galvani Drone combos are more reliable.
MB_EXPLOIT_STRATEGY.author: .bky_1556
MB_EXPLOIT_STRATEGY.context: bky distinguished between strategies optimized for raw win count versus win streaks against Master Bot.

MB_EXPLOIT_STRATEGY.insight: Using the CDS (Conduit-Drone-Steelsplitter or similar) opening as P1 against Master Bot is an effective strategy; MB responded with TR which was exploitable, and velizar_ was using an opening chart to systematically exploit MB's weaknesses.
MB_EXPLOIT_STRATEGY.author: velizar_
MB_EXPLOIT_STRATEGY.context: velizar_ reported using a community base set opening chart to consistently beat Master Bot as P1.

MB_EXPLOIT_STRATEGY.insight: A Steelsplitter float on turn 1 (instead of Militia + Drone) can be used specifically to force Master Bot to build a Wall instead of a Militia, manipulating its defensive decision-making in the opening.
MB_EXPLOIT_STRATEGY.author: stacko
MB_EXPLOIT_STRATEGY.context: stacko admitted the Splitter float opening was intentionally used to force Master Bot into building Wall instead of Militia.

MB_EXPLOIT_STRATEGY.insight: A player reported repeatedly losing to Master Bot when rushing large defenders (Omega Splitter and BBB-cost units) because they could not build damage afterward. The community confirmed that rushing BBB units is generally inadvisable and that Centurion is the best large-defender rush target due to being prompt and providing immediate attack.
MB_EXPLOIT_STRATEGY.author: consensus
MB_EXPLOIT_STRATEGY.context: totally_not_fbi reported losing to Master Bot while rushing big absorbers; community provided strategic correction.

MB_EXPLOIT_STRATEGY.insight: A useful learning technique against Master Bot is to replay a lost game with swapped positions: copy the line that beat you and let the bot play the line you should have played. This only works when the player is performing worse than Master Bot's level.
MB_EXPLOIT_STRATEGY.author: randomflyingtaco
MB_EXPLOIT_STRATEGY.context: Discussion of methods for learning from losses against Master Bot by replaying games with swapped sides.

MB_EXPLOIT_STRATEGY.insight: New players are advised to progress through bots in order of difficulty, finding the highest bot level they cannot easily beat and practicing until they can beat Master Bot before transitioning to ranked play. Master Bot is presented as the benchmark AI that, once beatable, indicates readiness for human competition.
MB_EXPLOIT_STRATEGY.author: redzonex
MB_EXPLOIT_STRATEGY.context: Advice to a new player on how to improve, recommending bot ladder as a training progression tool.

MB_EXPLOIT_STRATEGY.insight: A goose (Gauss Cannon) rush is sufficient to beat Master Bot, though this is acknowledged as not being a strong indicator of the strategy's true viability.
MB_EXPLOIT_STRATEGY.author: miuna6933
MB_EXPLOIT_STRATEGY.context: Discussion of whether goose rush is viable against Master Bot and what P1 should do in a Dynamo set.

MB_EXPLOIT_STRATEGY.insight: A recommended training method against Master Bot is to swap sides after losing: play the same set as P1 using the same general strategy the bot used, and compare the outcome. This reveals whether you lack the fundamentals to execute the strategy or whether you simply misread the set.
MB_EXPLOIT_STRATEGY.author: amalloy
MB_EXPLOIT_STRATEGY.context: amalloy suggested this training methodology to parchmentengineer as a way to learn from Master Bot losses.

MB_EXPLOIT_STRATEGY.insight: Adding Centrifuge and Zemora Voidbringer to the set (along with a unit like Blood Phage or Perforator) is a reliable method to beat Master Bot for achievements such as 'Comeback King', because the bot buys these units and wastes them, giving the human player a significant advantage.
MB_EXPLOIT_STRATEGY.author: nvlx
MB_EXPLOIT_STRATEGY.context: Community sharing strategies to earn the 'Comeback King' achievement (win after skipping a turn) against bots by exploiting MB's poor handling of Centrifuge and Zemora.

MB_EXPLOIT_STRATEGY.insight: A Perforator + Animus spam set as P1 is a known strategy to beat Master Bot, as MB frequently cannot defend against the early Perforator pressure; a user also won the 'Comeback King' achievement this way after skipping a turn.
MB_EXPLOIT_STRATEGY.author: artificialhope
MB_EXPLOIT_STRATEGY.context: Community advice on winning the 'Comeback King' achievement vs Master Bot; artificialhope recommended Perforator-only sets with Animus spam.

MB_EXPLOIT_STRATEGY.insight: Playing P2 into a Blood Phage rush, or a Steelforge rush (with Blastforge on turn 1), are reliable strategies to beat Master Bot and can be used to earn challenge achievements against bots.
MB_EXPLOIT_STRATEGY.author: nvlx
MB_EXPLOIT_STRATEGY.context: nvlx sharing personal experience of beating Master Bot for challenge achievements using Steelforge and Blood Phage rushes.

MB_EXPLOIT_STRATEGY.insight: Ultra-high economy sets are well-suited for earning the 'Comeback King' achievement against bots because the large resource buffer makes a skipped turn less costly, and bots struggle to capitalize on the tempo advantage.
MB_EXPLOIT_STRATEGY.author: shadourow
MB_EXPLOIT_STRATEGY.context: shadourow suggested ultra-high econ sets for the 'Comeback King' achievement and provided a replay as evidence.

MB_EXPLOIT_STRATEGY.insight: Master Bot can be beaten by the human player as P2 using a Drake strategy; the bot beats the human when it gets Drakes, but the human can counter by opening DDC and reacting flexibly rather than committing to Flame Animus.
MB_EXPLOIT_STRATEGY.author: zera777
MB_EXPLOIT_STRATEGY.context: zera777 asking for help after Master Bot beat them repeatedly with Drakes in a specific set.

MB_EXPLOIT_STRATEGY.insight: bky has a personal goal of achieving a 100-game win streak against Master Bot under standard conditions, suggesting Master Bot is consistently beatable by high-level players using principled play.
MB_EXPLOIT_STRATEGY.author: .bky_1556
MB_EXPLOIT_STRATEGY.context: bky mentioned mostly playing against Master Bot for dailies and aiming for a 100-streak under standard conditions.

MB_EXPLOIT_STRATEGY.insight: In Tia Threnody sets, buying Tia as early as the opponent can (essentially one turn before them) is described as sufficient to beat Master Bot, though experts note this heuristic is too simplistic for human opponents and there is no general shortcut for knowing when Tia is correct.
MB_EXPLOIT_STRATEGY.author: zera777
MB_EXPLOIT_STRATEGY.context: Discussion on Tia Threnody strategy, with zera claiming the 'get Tia first' heuristic beats Master Bot but amalloy and lyra cautioning it does not generalize.

MB_EXPLOIT_STRATEGY.insight: A Venge Cannon + Cluster Bolt + Galvani Drone rush as P1 with bait units added to the set is an effective strategy to beat Master Bot (and Adept Bot) for achievement purposes. Adding Mobile Animus to the set also helps.
MB_EXPLOIT_STRATEGY.author: lyra1712
MB_EXPLOIT_STRATEGY.context: Helping a player obtain bot-beating achievements using custom sets against Master Bot.

MB_EXPLOIT_STRATEGY.insight: For achievements requiring a high-econ win against bots, picking a custom set with strong defensive units (e.g. blue defenders) plus 5 random units is effective. Avoiding strong attacker units like Tatsu Nullifier and Cynestra in the random pool is important as they can disrupt the strategy.
MB_EXPLOIT_STRATEGY.author: lyra1712
MB_EXPLOIT_STRATEGY.context: Helping a player obtain achievement wins against Master Bot using custom sets.

MB_EXPLOIT_STRATEGY.insight: In the 'Extremely Unfair' challenge (opponent has 1 extra Drone as P2), the AI consistently opens with an early Blastforge on 2 Engineers. Exploiting this by opening Turn 3 Animus, buying 1 Auric Drone instead of 2 Drones to keep turns flexible, delaying your own Forge by one turn, using an early Rhino for defense, and then pumping Tarsiers—eventually buying out all 10—is a winning line against this bot behavior.
MB_EXPLOIT_STRATEGY.author: spiritfryer
MB_EXPLOIT_STRATEGY.context: spiritfryer's solution diary for the 'Extremely Unfair' combat scenario challenge, identifying consistent AI opening behavior and the line that exploits it.

MB_EXPLOIT_STRATEGY.insight: For the 'Unfair 2' challenge (opponent has +1 Engineer and Vivid Drone available), one winning line is the 'Elyot Animus' approach: buy 4 Vivid Drones as fast as possible, avoid wasting tech, avoid early Rhino (which gets abused by Militia), and eventually build blue for Iceblades against enemy Grimbotch.
MB_EXPLOIT_STRATEGY.author: pikachumemes.
MB_EXPLOIT_STRATEGY.context: pikachumemes sharing an alternative winning line for the 'Unfair 2' challenge, contrasting with spiritfryer's Apollo/Infusion Grid solution.

MB_EXPLOIT_STRATEGY.insight: Rushing Gauss Cannon / Blastforge is cited as the number one way to lose to Master Bot, implying MB is strong at defending or countering that specific rush strategy.
MB_EXPLOIT_STRATEGY.author: masn6811
MB_EXPLOIT_STRATEGY.context: masn6811 jokingly responded to 'always rush gauss fab' as the #1 way they lose to Master Bot.

MB_EXPLOIT_STRATEGY.insight: Including Venge Cannon in the set makes the Kappa challenge (a Master Bot variant challenge) very easy to beat, suggesting Master Bot struggles to handle Venge Cannon effectively in that context.
MB_EXPLOIT_STRATEGY.author: awaclus
MB_EXPLOIT_STRATEGY.context: awaclus made an offhand remark while p0lari mentioned Arkanishu playing 1-second Master Bot games, implying Venge Cannon trivializes the Kappa challenge against the bot.

MB_EXPLOIT_STRATEGY.insight: Master Bot can be beaten with a turn 1 Chrono Filter into turn 2 Hannibull opener, suggesting MB cannot adequately handle very aggressive or unconventional early-game lines that exploit its defensive weaknesses.
MB_EXPLOIT_STRATEGY.author: aa01blue
MB_EXPLOIT_STRATEGY.context: aa01blue mentioned this as an example of beating Master Bot with an unconventional line during a discussion about set analysis.

MB_EXPLOIT_STRATEGY.insight: A player explicitly sought community advice on how to beat Master Bot in a specific set, suggesting Master Bot has identifiable weaknesses exploitable through targeted set analysis and unit selection.
MB_EXPLOIT_STRATEGY.author: dextr0nic
MB_EXPLOIT_STRATEGY.context: Player asked for set advice specifically framed as a Master Bot challenge they were struggling to beat.

MB_EXPLOIT_STRATEGY.insight: A player learned how to counter Tarsier rush by trying it against Master Bot and observing how the bot countered it, suggesting Master Bot demonstrates viable anti-rush defensive patterns that can teach players correct responses.
MB_EXPLOIT_STRATEGY.author: 1m2o
MB_EXPLOIT_STRATEGY.context: Player comment on using Master Bot games as a learning tool for anti-rush defensive strategies.

MB_EXPLOIT_STRATEGY.insight: A P1 turn-2 Blood Phage rush was attempted against Master Bot, suggesting the bot may not adequately punish aggressive early red pressure builds.
MB_EXPLOIT_STRATEGY.author: amalloy
MB_EXPLOIT_STRATEGY.context: Casual mention during discussion about whether Tia Threnody is a rush unit, noting amalloy tried a P1 T2 Blood Phage against Master Bot.

MB_EXPLOIT_STRATEGY.insight: Against Master Bot in heavy red sets, opening with T1 Animus into aggressive pressure ('smorcus') can work because the bot does not respond optimally. However, this line loses to human opponents who respond with BED (Blastforge/Engineer/Drone) on T2.
MB_EXPLOIT_STRATEGY.author: siepu
MB_EXPLOIT_STRATEGY.context: siepu grinding daily rewards against Master Bot using an Animus rush and noting it works against the bot but not human responses like BED.

MB_EXPLOIT_STRATEGY.insight: cooledcannon uses Bloodrager over Tarsiers semi-often in low-inflation games against Master Bot, implying that the absorb-denial pattern is effective against the bot in those scenarios.
MB_EXPLOIT_STRATEGY.author: cooledcannon
MB_EXPLOIT_STRATEGY.context: cooledcannon mentioned using Bloodrager against Master Bot in a low-inflation context during a broader Bloodrager vs. Tarsier strategy discussion.

MB_EXPLOIT_STRATEGY.insight: crash_overlord is reported to have beaten Master Bot at 6-second bullet, suggesting the bot is beatable even under extreme time pressure with the right approach.
MB_EXPLOIT_STRATEGY.author: mrguy888
MB_EXPLOIT_STRATEGY.context: Casual mention during a discussion of bullet game formats and psychological tricks.

MB_FEATURE_REQUEST.insight: Players want Quick Play vs. Master Bot to award the same casual match trophies, so that experienced players who find casual match bots trivially easy are not forced to choose between earning rewards and playing challenging bot games.
MB_FEATURE_REQUEST.author: bifurcatedbitch
MB_FEATURE_REQUEST.context: bifurcatedbitch explaining the conflict between casual match trophy rewards and the superior bot quality available in Quick Play.

MB_FEATURE_REQUEST.insight: Community members and velizar_ specifically suggested creating a curated set of units that Master Bot plays well with, so that players could optionally generate games using only those units to get a more consistent and high-quality bot opponent.
MB_FEATURE_REQUEST.author: velizar_
MB_FEATURE_REQUEST.context: Discussion about Master Bot's highly variable performance across different unit sets, with velizar_ proposing a filtered set as a workaround.

MB_FEATURE_REQUEST.insight: A player requests that the bot be given a longer configurable time-per-turn option (e.g. 20 seconds) in addition to the existing 7-second option, and notes that BottyMcBotFace is difficult to get matched against in Casual Mode even with a win rate above 50% against it.
MB_FEATURE_REQUEST.author: velizar_
MB_FEATURE_REQUEST.context: Player requests stronger bot options after finding current bot settings too weak for practice.

MB_FEATURE_REQUEST.insight: Community members suggested that beating Master Bot (or Expert Bot) a set number of times should serve as an alternative skill-based unlock condition for ranked play, rather than requiring a pure grind.
MB_FEATURE_REQUEST.author: consensus
MB_FEATURE_REQUEST.context: Discussion about lowering the barrier to ranked play for new players, with p0lari, upskuttle, and jeacaveo agreeing on a bot-beating unlock alternative.

MB_FEATURE_REQUEST.insight: Community members have expressed a desire for Master Bot to concede earlier when in a clearly losing position, rather than prolonging games through excessive soaking with units like Blood Pact.
MB_FEATURE_REQUEST.author: consensus
MB_FEATURE_REQUEST.context: Both amalloy and masn6811 discussed the bot's late concession behavior, with masn6811 explicitly wishing it conceded slightly earlier.

MB_FEATURE_REQUEST.insight: A community member flagged that the Prismata bot had become out of date, implying it no longer reflects current game balance or strategies.
MB_FEATURE_REQUEST.author: apooche
MB_FEATURE_REQUEST.context: apooche notifying a developer (Wiwiweb) that the bot is outdated.

MB_FEATURE_REQUEST.insight: Community members want an analysis-mode button that retrieves the Master Bot's recommended move for the current board state without leaving analysis mode.
MB_FEATURE_REQUEST.author: apooche
MB_FEATURE_REQUEST.context: apooche suggested the feature while discussing analysis tools, with deadhour agreeing it would be useful.

MB_FEATURE_REQUEST.insight: Players want the ability to pause or resume an in-progress bot game when a ranked match becomes available, rather than being forced to abandon the bot game. This feature is considered non-trivial to implement.
MB_FEATURE_REQUEST.author: jeacaveo
MB_FEATURE_REQUEST.context: jeacaveo raised the request when experiencing the conflict between queuing for ranked and playing a bot game, noting they intended to submit the suggestion in-game.

MB_FEATURE_REQUEST.insight: Community members have requested that Master Bot be improved to: (1) correctly hold Doomed Drones on 1 lifespan for defense, (2) understand and execute absorb denial, (3) understand granularity denial, and (4) correctly handle freeze units. These are viewed as the most impactful gaps in MB's play.
MB_FEATURE_REQUEST.author: consensus
MB_FEATURE_REQUEST.context: Extended discussion across multiple threads about MB weaknesses and what improvements would most benefit the bot's play quality.

MB_FEATURE_REQUEST.insight: Community members noted that Master Bot becomes more challenging when the player sets their timer very low, suggesting that time pressure is the primary mechanism by which Master Bot difficulty scales for experienced players rather than the bot's decision quality improving.
MB_FEATURE_REQUEST.author: p0lari
MB_FEATURE_REQUEST.context: Discussion about how hard Master Bot is for experienced players, with p0lari noting the timer as a difficulty lever.

MB_FEATURE_REQUEST.insight: Community members expressed interest in having more distinct AI opponents at slightly higher skill levels than existing bots, as a way to help players bridge the gap between Master Bot and multiplayer.
MB_FEATURE_REQUEST.author: anesotericman
MB_FEATURE_REQUEST.context: Discussion about improving the new player experience included suggestions for stronger intermediate bots as an alternative to immediately entering ranked queue.

MB_FEATURE_REQUEST.insight: Master Bot needs to learn to properly use Infusion Grid; the community notes it fails to utilize this unit correctly.
MB_FEATURE_REQUEST.author: velizar_
MB_FEATURE_REQUEST.context: velizar_ posted a brief complaint that Master Bot needs to use Infusion Grid.

MB_FEATURE_REQUEST.insight: Community members have requested a 'Grandmaster Bot' that uses stronger AI, as Master Bot is beatable at a very high rate even by top players and does not provide a sufficient challenge.
MB_FEATURE_REQUEST.author: consensus
MB_FEATURE_REQUEST.context: Discussion about Master Bot's weakness led to community suggestions for a stronger AI opponent.

MB_FEATURE_REQUEST.insight: Community members requested that beating Master Bot consistently should count toward or unlock ranked rating progression (e.g., allowing players to reach 1500 rating via bot games), with suggestions to raise or remove the current 500 MMR cap on bot games.
MB_FEATURE_REQUEST.author: zezetel
MB_FEATURE_REQUEST.context: zezetel suggested that consistently beating Master Bot should qualify players for a 1500 rating floor, and silentslayers suggested raising the MMR cap for bot games.

MB_FEATURE_REQUEST.insight: Community members suggest Master Bot could be significantly improved by: adding an opening book keyed to the top units in each random set, using an AlphaGo-style approach to generate candidate moves and evaluate board states, and improving the set of algorithms considered for each sub-turn phase.
MB_FEATURE_REQUEST.author: consensus
MB_FEATURE_REQUEST.context: Extended discussion among apooche, spiritfryer, and wonderboat about how Master Bot works and what would make it stronger.

MB_FEATURE_REQUEST.insight: A community member mentioned that one of the game developer Dave's graduate students was working on improving Master Bot, but no changes were expected in the near term as of mid-2018.
MB_FEATURE_REQUEST.author: mtanzer
MB_FEATURE_REQUEST.context: Discussion about the possibility of improving Master Bot using machine learning approaches similar to AlphaZero/Leela Zero.

MB_FEATURE_REQUEST.insight: Community members expressed desire for a continuous difficulty slider for bots rather than discrete levels (Basic/Adept/Expert/Master), noting that even Master 7s is not challenging for many experienced players, and that a deep-learning bot with difficulty scaling would be ideal.
MB_FEATURE_REQUEST.author: velizar_
MB_FEATURE_REQUEST.context: Discussion comparing Master Bot difficulty to chess bots, with velizar_ and sano712 debating whether the existing difficulty levels are sufficient.

MB_FEATURE_REQUEST.insight: velizar_ suggested creating and publishing a curated list of units that Master Bot plays well with, so players can construct custom game sets optimized for bot play — noting that the bot is specifically bad with Zemora Voidbringer and Mobile Animus but that identifying its strengths is unclear.
MB_FEATURE_REQUEST.author: velizar_
MB_FEATURE_REQUEST.context: velizar_ proposed a community resource to help players enjoy games against Master Bot by using sets the bot handles well.

MB_FEATURE_REQUEST.insight: A community member (.sha6001) requested a bot stronger than Master Bot at depth 7, suggesting the current Master Bot ceiling is not sufficient for experienced players.
MB_FEATURE_REQUEST.author: .sha6001
MB_FEATURE_REQUEST.context: Discussion about sniping Xaetron, during which sha requested a harder bot than Master Bot depth 7.

MB_FEATURE_REQUEST.insight: masn6811 asked whether there is a way to play event/MOTW (Month of the Week) sets against Master Bot, implying this functionality was not available or not obvious at the time.
MB_FEATURE_REQUEST.author: masn6811
MB_FEATURE_REQUEST.context: masn6811 missed a live event and asked if Master Bot could be used to play event sets instead.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: The casual match bot pool contains bots (e.g., EpiCRusheR) that are far weaker than Master Bot and essentially provide non-games for intermediate-to-advanced players, while Master Bot remains the only bot offering a meaningful challenge in that skill range.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: bifurcatedbitch
MB_WEAKNESS.context: bifurcatedbitch explaining why they prefer Quick Play over Casual Match due to bot quality differences.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Master Bot's skill varies hugely depending on the unit set in play; on some sets it plays reasonably well, but on others it effectively throws the game. This makes its difficulty highly inconsistent across games.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: consensus
MB_WEAKNESS.context: Discussion about how hard it is for new players to reach 50% winrate against Master Bot, with sano712 and velizar_ noting the bot's performance varies wildly by set.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Master Bot can be exploited by offering a gambit that any human would accept but the bot declines, or by setting up a large purchase that only works if the AI buys a Wall instead of a Steelsplitter — which it reliably does even when a Steelsplitter would block the attack anyway.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: masn6811
MB_WEAKNESS.context: Discussion about whether a heuristic-heavy bot would be useful, with masn6811 citing specific known exploitable patterns in the existing AI.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: The Master Bot (and AI generally) cannot handle pay-gold-for-attack click abilities on units like the proposed Smorcus rework because it can create circular resource dependencies the AI's decision engine cannot resolve correctly.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: elyot
MB_WEAKNESS.context: elyot explaining why pay-gold-for-attack abilities are avoided in design due to the AI's inability to handle circular resource dependencies.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Cryo Ray's bot (Master Bot) behavior of wasting freeze clicks on suboptimal targets was noted as a known problem; a lifespan-based Cryo Ray redesign was partially motivated by the observation that it would at least prevent the bot from misusing it to freeze things that aren't worth freezing.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: velizar_
MB_WEAKNESS.context: Community discussion about redesigning Cryo Ray, with velizar_ noting a silver lining that lifespan would mitigate the bot's poor freeze targeting.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Even after banning units the bot is specifically weak with, velizar_ reports the bot still does not play well enough to be a meaningful opponent, suggesting the bot has broad strategic weaknesses beyond unit-specific ones.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: velizar_
MB_WEAKNESS.context: Player reports that banning bot-exploitable units did not significantly improve bot performance.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: The Master Bot can be beaten even when given a 5-gold handicap in some sets, and steel0229e notes that in certain sets you can beat Master Bot by skipping the first turn entirely, suggesting the bot has set-dependent exploitable weaknesses significant enough to overcome large handicaps.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: steel0229e
MB_WEAKNESS.context: Discussion about whether the 'Unfair Challenge' (bot gets an extra drone) and the 5-gold-handicap achievement are still beatable.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Master Bot does not use Amporilla (referred to as 'ampo'), which may cause new players to underestimate the unit initially since they won't face it from the AI.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: 307th
MB_WEAKNESS.context: Discussion about which units new players would complain about after release, with 307th noting masterbot doesn't use ampo so players might like it at first.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Master Bot plays poorly against Electrovore and against tier-2 blue opening strategies, which are known exploitable weaknesses even though the bot is considered strong relative to other game AIs.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: crash_overlord
MB_WEAKNESS.context: Community discussion about Master Bot difficulty and how good it is compared to AIs in other games, with crash_overlord and randomflyingtaco calling out specific weaknesses.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Master Bot makes poor strategic decisions in certain unit sets, such as going 2 Engineers into a Defense Grid set, demonstrating it is weak at high-level gameplan selection even if competent at low-level tactics and execution.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: gadget246
MB_WEAKNESS.context: Player quoted a common claim that Master Bot is a very difficult opponent, then gave a concrete counterexample of it making an obviously bad opening in a specific set.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Prismata AI (including casual bots) is described as good at low-level tactics and execution but poor at selecting the correct gameplan for a given random set. Beating casual bots becomes harder when the unit count is reduced (fewer units in the set), because the AI struggles more with gameplan selection in richer sets where it can default to familiar patterns.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: p0lari
MB_WEAKNESS.context: p0lari shared personal experience of switching from maximum to minimum unit count in casual bot matches and starting to occasionally lose, attributing this to the AI being bad at picking the right gameplan.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Master Bot is notably weaker with units requiring heavy tech costs (three or more of the same color in a unit's icon), and struggles to find optimal lines involving complex units like Lucina (not in unit list, treat as historical reference) or Odin-style strategies, while performing best with straightforward vanilla unit sets.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: masn6811
MB_WEAKNESS.context: Discussion about Master Bot's strengths and weaknesses across different set compositions.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Master Bot is strongest in low-economy tactical mirrors (e.g., Grimbotch sets) where precise arithmetic matters, because it excels at calculation; however, it is weaker at high-level strategic decisions such as identifying powerful unit synergies or complex multi-color tech lines.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: consensus
MB_WEAKNESS.context: mtanzer noted MB is scariest in low-econ tactical mirrors; namington attributed this to MB being better at tactics than overall strategy.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Master Bot is generally considered not strong by expert players; mtanzer explicitly states 'master bot isn't good, it's possible to do a lot better than mbot for sure', suggesting skilled players can exploit its limited move-generation heuristics.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: mtanzer
MB_WEAKNESS.context: Community discussion about the quality of Master Bot relative to top human players and the potential for a stronger AI.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Master Bot does not know how to take full advantage of extra gold in the early game; giving MB a 5-gold handicap is reportedly easier than giving the same handicap to Adept Bot, which still plays sensibly when you skip your first turn.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: awaclus
MB_WEAKNESS.context: Players comparing difficulty of handicap games against Master Bot versus Adept Bot.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Players prefer VS Computer mode over casual queue bot games specifically to ban units that cause Master Bot to make poor decisions, implying there are known unit types or sets where MB plays especially badly.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: randomflyingtaco
MB_WEAKNESS.context: Discussion about matchmaking options and why some players prefer the VS Computer lobby over casual queue when playing against bots.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: The weaker AI opponents on the casual ladder (not Master Bot) predictably pursue simple strategies like huge economy or all-in early red rushes, making them useful for learning how to counter those specific strategies, but not representative of high-level play.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: shadourow
MB_WEAKNESS.context: Discussion about the value of playing against AI opponents in the casual ladder as a learning tool for new players.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: The bots (including pacifist bot and others) consistently exhibit a known behavioral pattern that has been reproducible for a long time, suggesting systematic weaknesses in how they handle certain board states. When the AI (PrismataAI.exe) times out or returns an illegal move, the Wacky Bot built into the client takes over seamlessly.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: apooche
MB_WEAKNESS.context: Discussion about bot architecture: PrismataAI.exe handles MB/Adept/BottyMcBotFace, while Wacky Bot is a client-side fallback when the external AI fails.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Master Bot is known to respond poorly to an early Animus rush: in one observed game it bought a Centrifuge (costing blue and 4 red) on the same turn as the opponent's turn-1 Animus, wasting significant resources and falling well behind.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: vanitascabal6962
