MB_WEAKNESS.context: Player noted that Master Bot responded to their turn-1 Animus by buying a Centrifuge on turn 2, squandering blue and 4 red, and the rush achievement was trivially easy as a result.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Master Bot does not handle super fast rushes well, which partially compensates for its inherent advantage in the 'Totally Unfair' challenge scenario where it starts with a winning position on turn 1.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: p0lari
MB_WEAKNESS.context: Discussion about strategies for beating Master Bot in the Totally Unfair challenge, with p0lari noting fast rushes as an effective equalizer.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: The 2016 version of Master Bot had hardcoded Vivid Drone openings, but Vivid Drone was reworked in 2017, making those hardcoded lines outdated and potentially suboptimal or incorrect for the reworked unit.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: apooche
MB_WEAKNESS.context: apooche noted that Master Bot's Vivid Drone opening logic was hardcoded to pre-rework behavior, making it stale after the 2017 rework.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: A player around 1800 Master rating noted they still occasionally lose to Master Bot when not playing carefully, indicating Master Bot's strength is high enough to punish inattentive play even from experienced players.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: redzonex
MB_WEAKNESS.context: Community discussion about the relative strength of Prismata bots for new players; redzonex provided personal context on MB's competitive level.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Master Bot plays at approximately low Masters or Tier 8/9 level according to community assessment; a Tier 6 player reports being able to beat it consistently, suggesting it is beatable by intermediate players.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: namington
MB_WEAKNESS.context: A new player asked how hard Master Bot is; namington and xujhan both weighed in on its skill level.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Master Bot handles certain unit combinations very poorly; p0lari initially believed it was just bad at units like Immaculon, but realized the /RotatingInferno command was actually switching it to Wacky Bot mode, meaning the apparent weakness may be a mode-switch rather than a genuine strategic flaw in Master Bot.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: p0lari
MB_WEAKNESS.context: p0lari discussed their experience dunking on the bot with Immaculon and a Fire Spinner, then discovered /RotatingInferno was the cause.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Master Bot concedes significantly later than optimal, often continuing play 2+ turns after the outcome is clear to experienced players. It has been observed attempting quintuple Blood Pact while behind by approximately 10 attack, only conceding after the first major breach or a turn after.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: amalloy
MB_WEAKNESS.context: amalloy described consistent personal experience of MB refusing to concede in clearly lost positions, with a specific example of quintuple Blood Pact soaking.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Master Bot does not know base set only (BSO) lines at all; it appears to nat conduit, which always loses except against a Master Bot blastforge opening, and even then it likely plays poorly.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: namington
MB_WEAKNESS.context: Discussion of whether BSO lines could be tested against Master Bot to verify community analysis.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Master Bot plays very poorly with Ebb Turbine, buying 4 Ebb Turbines and clicking them all every turn to churn through its entire drone supply, then rebuying the drones — a deeply inefficient loop.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: amalloy
MB_WEAKNESS.context: amalloy observed Master Bot's behavior in a game featuring Ebb Turbine and described the specific mistake pattern.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Master Bot generally struggles with knowing when to stop buying drones and start buying attack units. The Ebb Turbine case is an extension of this broader weakness: by consuming the drone supply through Ebb Turbine clicks, the bot sidesteps its own economy-to-attack transition problem.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: namington
MB_WEAKNESS.context: namington followed up amalloy's Ebb Turbine observation with a general explanation of Master Bot's drone/attack timing weakness.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Master Bot's effective skill level is highly set-dependent: on some sets it plays at roughly 2200 MMR equivalence, while on others it drops below 1200 MMR. When it makes a mistake on a given set, it repeats that mistake every single time due to its deterministic nature.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: .jrkirby
MB_WEAKNESS.context: jrkirby discussed using Master Bot for practice and noted the extreme variance in bot quality across different unit sets.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Master Bot misdefends in positions involving a Xaetron as absorber alongside lifespan-1 units. When incoming damage exceeds the Doomed Wall's absorb, it prioritizes the Xaetron as the primary absorber and then sacrifices the lifespan-1 Doomed Wall as soak first, rather than absorbing on the Doomed Wall directly — resulting in unnecessary damage to the Xaetron.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: amalloy
MB_WEAKNESS.context: amalloy observed Master Bot making a specific misdefense with a Xaetron and lifespan-1 Doomed Wall, and proposed a theory for why the bot's logic goes wrong.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: The Q (auto-defend) button is known to mishandle defense when a Xaetron is present, sometimes trying to sacrifice the Xaetron unnecessarily. Expert players recommend never using Q when Xaetron is on board, or always checking and correcting the auto-defense.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: apooche
MB_WEAKNESS.context: apooche warned that the auto-defend Q button is not designed to handle Xaetron correctly, and that Ctrl-click can also attempt to sacrifice Xaetron when unnecessary.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Master Bot nearly forced a draw in a Savior mirror game but threw the win by killing the opponent's last Savior instead of their last Drone, indicating it struggles with correct target prioritization in high-economy endgame positions.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: amalloy
MB_WEAKNESS.context: amalloy reported being surprised that Master Bot nearly won a Savior mirror, but noted it threw the game by misidentifying the correct target to kill.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Master Bot's play level fluctuates significantly between games, with some games producing strong builds and others being much weaker. It is generally considered poor at high-economy situations such as Savior mirrors.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: consensus
MB_WEAKNESS.context: amalloy and crash_overlord discussed Master Bot's inconsistency, with amalloy noting it surprised him in a Savior mirror and crash_overlord attributing variance to 'lucky builds'.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Master Bot's ranked performance peaked at Tier 6 (48% toward Tier 7), indicating it plays at approximately a Tier 5-6 human level. It has not overcome the difficulty peak of the ranked player distribution, making it consistently beatable by mid-to-high tier ranked players.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: mrguy888
MB_WEAKNESS.context: mrguy888 and amalloy discussing Master Bot's historical ranked performance, with amalloy citing the AAAI paper and mrguy888 noting MB has not gotten over the bell curve.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Master Bot has no coherent strategy for Savior and makes unintelligible plays whenever the unit is in the set. It cannot handle Savior's build-time correctly, failing to recognize the unit is on the board while it has build time remaining.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: apooche
MB_WEAKNESS.context: Community discussion about Master Bot's poor handling of Savior, confirmed by multiple players including apooche.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Master Bot does not properly account for build time on units it has purchased, failing to plan around them while they are still being constructed. This is a general architectural limitation rather than a unit-specific bug.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: kristian1103
MB_WEAKNESS.context: Discussion about Master Bot buying Vivid Drones after Savior, with kristian1103 suggesting the root cause is that MB doesn't recognize units on the board during build time.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: The Q defense algorithm (auto-defend) in Prismata has a known weakness with fragile units: it will absorb damage onto a Tia Threnody instead of an Infusion Grid, which is incorrect since losing Tia costs too much defense tempo compared to losing the Infusion Grid.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: jamberine
MB_WEAKNESS.context: Players discussing cases where the Q defense auto-assign makes suboptimal choices with fragile units like Tia Threnody.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: The Q defense algorithm will incorrectly absorb damage onto an Odin instead of a Xaetron when forced to choose between the two, whereas back-lining on Xaetron is almost always clearly correct since Odin has typically already been clicked if the player wanted to defend with it.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: amalloy
MB_WEAKNESS.context: amalloy responding to Terra Reveene about the Q defense algorithm's decision-making between Odin and Xaetron absorption.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: The Q defense algorithm can sacrifice a Steelsplitter to keep a Wall alive, which is a clear misdefense — it should save the Steelsplitter over the Wall in most situations.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: amalloy
MB_WEAKNESS.context: amalloy recounting a personal experience where Q defense soaked with a Splitter to leave a Wall alive, which is the wrong priority.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: The Q defense algorithm can incorrectly sacrifice Forcefields over Walls when multiple defenders are present — it may lose 3 Forcefields over 2 Walls, which is usually the wrong choice since Forcefields are more valuable defenders.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: apooche
MB_WEAKNESS.context: apooche explaining specific failure modes of Q defense to sorawotobu, including losing Forcefields over Walls.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: The Q defense algorithm can unnecessarily sacrifice granularity: when a player has a mix of Infusion units and Walls, it may lose an Infusion and 2 Engineers instead of the correct 2 Walls, needlessly giving up granular attack value.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: apooche
MB_WEAKNESS.context: apooche detailing Q defense failure modes where it misallocates absorb between Infusions and Walls.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: The bot (implied AI opponent) is very bad at handling freeze mechanics, appearing to always use freeze even when there is no exploit opportunity or breach situation, wasting the freeze ability.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: sonoja
MB_WEAKNESS.context: Discussion about granularity and exploit mechanics led to a side comment about bot behavior with freeze.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Master Bot is described as weaker than many human players, making it an unreliable benchmark for testing strategies; winning a strategy against Master Bot does not validate that strategy against skilled human opponents.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: f300xen
MB_WEAKNESS.context: f300xen cautioned artificialhope that success against Master Bot with the Animus/Perforator rush does not prove the strategy is sound.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Master Bot mishandles Lifespan units such as Grimbotch: it recognizes that having attack is better than not having attack, so it buys Grimbotches, but then fails to click them against the opponent's defense grid because it cannot search deep enough ahead to see their value, causing it to simply hold them unused.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: amalloy
MB_WEAKNESS.context: Discussion in #ask_a_dev about why Master Bot plays Grimbotch worse than even a weak human player.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Master Bot does not search very many turns ahead, causing it to treat Lifespan units as if they were persistent units (e.g., treating Grimbotch like a Tarsier). This shallow search is a systemic weakness affecting any unit whose value depends on future sequencing.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: amalloy
MB_WEAKNESS.context: amalloy confirmed Zera's hypothesis that Master Bot treats Grimbotch like a Tarsier due to limited lookahead depth.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Master Bot does not understand the value of absorbing damage, suggesting it fails to optimally assign incoming damage to blockers/defenders to preserve key units.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: awaclus
MB_WEAKNESS.context: awaclus made an offhand comment that someone should tell Master Bot that absorbing is good, implying the bot undervalues defensive absorption play.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Master Bot often reaches max absorb very quickly, meaning it rapidly builds up a large economy and can absorb a lot of incoming damage, which is a notable behavioral pattern players should account for.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: amalloy
MB_WEAKNESS.context: Brief observation about Master Bot's economic behavior in games.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Master Bot heavily favors buying Tarsiers as its primary attack unit, to the point where it is predictable and can be exploited by players who build economies and tech buildings while the bot floods Tarsiers.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: zera777
MB_WEAKNESS.context: Zera humorously described Master Bot spamming Tarsiers while they built up a full tech economy, with community agreement that the bot is predictable.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Master Bot is susceptible to absorb denial: if a player opens with a unit like Flame Animus into Auride Core, the bot will always respond by buying a Wall instead of an attacker, even when buying an attacker would be stronger.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: amalloy
MB_WEAKNESS.context: Discussion of specific exploitable Master Bot behaviors that give knowledgeable players an edge even when those plays are suboptimal against strong human opponents.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Master Bot always spends all of its flexible attack as long as doing so kills at least one Engineer, even if that means wasting many attackers (e.g., blowing 10 Pixies to kill one Engineer). Players can exploit this by holding back a unit like Chieftain and absorbing on Wall, baiting the bot into inefficient attacks.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: amalloy
MB_WEAKNESS.context: amalloy listing specific predictable Master Bot behaviors that can be exploited by players who know the bot's patterns in advance.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Master Bot never gambits (makes sacrificial early aggressive plays), which is a known exploitable pattern. Players who are aware of this can safely ignore gambit-defense considerations and optimize their opening purely for economy and standard play.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: siepu
MB_WEAKNESS.context: Discussion of what players know about Master Bot's behavior that gives them an advantage when aware they are facing it.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Master Bot cannot purchase technology buildings (tech units), which severely limits its strategic options and makes it predictable in sets where tech units are key. Top players estimate their winrate against Master Bot at roughly 90-95%, with losses mostly attributable to misreading the set rather than the bot outplaying them.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: consensus
MB_WEAKNESS.context: Extended community discussion about Master Bot's strength level, with amalloy estimating ~90-95% winrate and mrguy888 arguing top players should be near 100% when trying.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Master Bot tends to rush Gauss Cannons whenever Thorium Dynamo is in the set, and heavily favors breachproof defensive strategies (Walls, Forcefields), often ignoring more complex advanced units in the set.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: stacko
MB_WEAKNESS.context: velizar_ shared multiple replays of Master Bot repeating the same Thorium Dynamo into Gauss Cannon rush pattern, with stacko confirming this as a known bot tendency.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Master Bot can lose to its own simple Gauss Cannon / Iso Kronus / Thorium Dynamo rush strategies when the human player correctly responds with base-set fundamentals, suggesting the bot does not adapt its strategy to the set's advanced units.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: velizar_
MB_WEAKNESS.context: velizar_ posted replays where they lost to Master Bot's repetitive Gauss rush lines, noting the bot ignores advanced units like Arka.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Master Bot ignores advanced units like Arka even when they are available and strategically relevant, defaulting instead to a simple Fabricator + Gauss Cannon rush.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: velizar_
MB_WEAKNESS.context: velizar_ noted in a replay share that the AI ignored Arka entirely and still won with a Fabricator + Gauss rush.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Master Bot exhibits exploitable behavior with frontline units in sets containing Feral and Polywall Shredder, specifically offering opportunities to deny absorb by attacking frontline units. This weakness is noted in the context of the 'Extremely Unfair Challenge' bot game.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: sano712
MB_WEAKNESS.context: Discussion of strategies to beat the Extremely Unfair Challenge against the bot, where sano notes the bot offers multiple exploits with its frontline units.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Master Bot struggles to respond correctly to aggressive Animus-based openings (e.g., A/11/11/11/11 as P1) in Base+Immolite sets, allowing this line to be exploited for the 'Kappa' achievement badge.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: shadourow
MB_WEAKNESS.context: Discussion about which badges are hardest to earn and how to exploit MB for the Kappa badge.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: In Red Base (Animus + Tarsier only, no Drones or Engineers), Master Bot as P2 appears much harder to beat than as P1, suggesting the bot plays near-optimally in that specific constrained setup when it has the second-player advantage, but human P2 likely wins with a fastimus line against MB as P1.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: amalloy
MB_WEAKNESS.context: Discussion of hyreon's attempts to beat Master Bot in Red Base only as P1, with amalloy and hyreon analyzing P1/P2 asymmetry.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Master Bot does not understand stamina as a resource, leading it to make incorrect absorb decisions. Specifically, it values a 0-stamina Bombarder over an Energy Matrix, and will absorb on a 0-stamina Corpus over a Wall, implying it also absorbs on 1- or 2-stamina Corpus over Wall, which is almost always incorrect.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: chlobes
MB_WEAKNESS.context: Discussion of Master Bot's strategic weaknesses related to stamina-based units and absorb decisions.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Master Bot has very bad strategic ideas in many games, though it can still defend accurately and take a normal amount of time to plan turns. Its weaknesses are well-known to experienced players and do not require developer intervention to identify.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: amalloy
MB_WEAKNESS.context: Response to a player asking a dev about Master Bot behavior, with amalloy noting MB's strategic weakness is a known pattern, not a bug.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Master Bot misuses Mobile Animus by purchasing excessive quantities (up to 9) and converting them all into Rhinos while wasting the red mana, leaving itself with almost no permanent attack and losing the game.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: velizar_
MB_WEAKNESS.context: velizar_ observed MB buying 9 Mobile Animuses and converting them all to Rhinos while wasting red, prompting a broader discussion about correct Rhino usage.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Master Bot plays poorly in sets with high-defense or many defensive units, as it struggles to handle very high economy situations where the correct response requires complex defensive buildup.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: shadourow
MB_WEAKNESS.context: shadourow noted that adding all defense units to a set is a reliable way to rig Master Bot achievements because MB is very bad at very high econ games.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Master Bot has never been observed successfully firing a Zemora Voidbringer; if MB buys Zemora, the player has likely already won. MB also does not appear to understand that Zemora requires Gaussite to fire, as it ignores opponent Conduits (the Gaussite source) and targets Venge Cannons instead.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: jamberine
MB_WEAKNESS.context: Discussion about the attack potential UI showing +8 for Zemora even without Gaussite sources led to observations about MB's inability to play Zemora correctly.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Master Bot treats Wild Drone as a blocker rather than recognizing it as a snipe target, making it easy to exploit in sets containing Wild Drone. However, MB would still prefer to breach rather than snipe a Wild Drone.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: cronos
MB_WEAKNESS.context: cronos stated MB treats Wild Drone as a blocker, with velizar_ adding the nuance that MB would still prefer breach over Wild Drone snipe.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Master Bot exhibits poor resource management in endgame scenarios: in a set with Thorium Dynamo and Gauss Cannon it pursued a breach-proof strategy, then after Gauss Cannons ran out of supply it bought Engineers/Drones/Forcefields, and when Engineers ran out it floated all gold, ending the game with 51 unspent gold instead of buying Conduits to convert excess gold into more resources.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: velizar_
MB_WEAKNESS.context: velizar_ described a specific game where MB floated 51 gold at game end rather than buying Conduits to spend it productively.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Master Bot does not correctly evaluate the threat posed by Zemora Voidbringer in relation to the opponent's Gaussite supply: on turn 9 it ignored the opponent's Conduits (the Gaussite source enabling Zemora) and targeted Venge Cannons instead, missing a narrow winning line that required destroying the Conduits.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: velizar_
MB_WEAKNESS.context: velizar_ analyzed a specific game where MB's failure to prioritize Conduit destruction over Venge Cannons nearly cost it the game against a Zemora strategy.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Master Bot plays Gauss Charge poorly, as observed by multiple players. Velizar confirmed it is one of several units the bot mishandles, and p0lari noted it buying double Gauss Charges into a Wall as nonsensical behavior.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: velizar_
MB_WEAKNESS.context: p0lari complained about Master Bot buying double Gauss Charges into their Wall, and velizar_ confirmed Gauss Charge is one of the units Master Bot is known to be bad with.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Master Bot is bad with Kinetic, specifically using it the very next turn after buying it to snipe a Conduit rather than saving it for more impactful use. This was observed with replay code 1o0XS-SbPpw.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: velizar_
MB_WEAKNESS.context: Velizar_ shared a replay where Master Bot immediately used Kinetic the turn after purchasing it to snipe a Conduit, identifying this as a known bot weakness.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Master Bot is limited to evaluating positions roughly 2 turns ahead; it occasionally buys units that would have been more useful earlier, effectively thinking '-1 turns ahead'. It also tends to stop defending entirely once it determines it cannot defend everything.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: consensus
MB_WEAKNESS.context: Multiple players including zera777 and amalloy discussed Master Bot's shallow lookahead and its tendency to abandon defense entirely when overwhelmed, in the context of a game velizar_ shared where the bot threw a winning position.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Master Bot has a known tendency to make suboptimal blocking decisions late-game, such as clicking Rhino in a way that allows a Corpus breach, and appears to assume Zemora Voidbringer always fires, which can cause premature resignation in otherwise defensible positions.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: velizar_
MB_WEAKNESS.context: During analysis of a bot game replay, velizar_ and jeacaveo observed Master Bot clicking Rhino to allow a Corpus breach and suspected the bot always assumes Zemora fires when evaluating the position.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Master Bot playing as 'Fastimus' always buys an early Animus regardless of the set, making it a weaker and predictable opponent compared to Master Bot proper.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: amalloy
MB_WEAKNESS.context: jeacaveo asked whether Fastimus was a Master Bot variant; amalloy clarified it is a weaker bot that always buys an early Animus.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Master Bot over-buys tech structures in complex multi-tech sets without coordinating them effectively; for example it was observed buying double Blastforge for Doomed Mech, double Animus for Lucina, Flame Animus for attack and red generation, and Conduit for Tantalum Ray and Forcefields all at once, suggesting it does not prune redundant tech well.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: velizar_
MB_WEAKNESS.context: velizar_ described a game where Master Bot was buying an overloaded tech base including double Blastforge, double Animus, Flame Animus, and Conduit, characterising it as normal but suboptimal bot behavior.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Master Bot wastes tech resources by buying a tech building on one turn and then failing to spend the colored resources it produces on subsequent turns, indicating poor long-term planning around tech investments.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: awaclus
MB_WEAKNESS.context: flakmaniak observed MB buying double Blastforge early but waiting until turn 10 to buy Centurion, wasting blue in the interim.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Master Bot plays poorly with Doomed Drone: it does not hold Doomed Drones on 1 lifespan for defensive purposes nearly enough, and will instead build Forcefields when holding a Doomed Drone would be more efficient. This was confirmed by multiple players.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: flakmaniak
MB_WEAKNESS.context: flakmaniak observed the pattern across multiple games as P2 with Doomed Drone in the set; wilson0862 confirmed it.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Master Bot cannot effectively defend against Smorcus rushes, including both a 'crazimus' Smorcus rush and an 'Elyot animus' Smorcus rush. In Smorcus sets, the bot defaults to a fast Animus build even when a Drone Grid is available, which masn6811 identifies as a mistake.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: masn6811
MB_WEAKNESS.context: masn6811 describing specific MB weaknesses in a discussion about which units to ban when practicing against Master Bot.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Master Bot only uses 'Q defense' (blocking optimally to minimize damage taken) and never accepts non-obvious gambits. It assumes the human player will also Q-defend, making it exploitable by sacrificial attacks such as Rhino-onto-Wall or Wall-onto-Rhino gambits that a human would accept but MB will not, even if accepting would let it breach the opponent's Drone Grid.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: masn6811
MB_WEAKNESS.context: masn6811 explaining the bot's defensive decision-making model and how it creates exploitable patterns with gambit attacks.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Master Bot does not understand the concept of denying absorb: it will not use attack-costing units, click Thermite Core, or activate Auric Impulse specifically to deny an opponent's absorb opportunity. It also does not deny granularity (e.g., it will not hold a Steelsplitter to prevent an opponent from buying a key unit at an awkward resource level).
MB_WEAKNESS.author: masn6811
MB_WEAKNESS.context: masn6811 and velizar_ discussing the bot's inability to exploit absorb denial and granularity denial as offensive concepts.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Master Bot's exploit detection algorithm clicks all available attackers, then checks if it can unclick some while still achieving the same breach result. This means it can find simple exploits but misses complex ones requiring suboptimal immediate damage to set up future breaches, or exploits that require holding units strategically.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: p0lari
MB_WEAKNESS.context: p0lari describing the exact algorithmic approach Master Bot uses for exploit decisions during the bot weakness discussion.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Master Bot plays poorly with all freeze units: it wastes freeze abilities and misses opportunities to exploit opponent defenses using freeze. It is particularly poor with limited-use freeze units, slightly better with continuous freeze like Iceblade.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: velizar_
MB_WEAKNESS.context: velizar_ listing MB weaknesses by unit type in a discussion about which units to ban when using MB as a practice opponent.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Master Bot does not buy Infusion Grid even when it is the strategically correct purchase. velizar_ notes it is a good unit that the bot should buy but doesn't.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: velizar_
MB_WEAKNESS.context: velizar_ commenting on specific units the bot fails to purchase correctly during a discussion of MB weaknesses and which units to ban for bot practice.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Master Bot snipes opponent Conduits with Kinetic Driver too early (before it is strategically correct), wasting the sniping ability on targets that do not justify the tempo loss at that point in the game.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: velizar_
MB_WEAKNESS.context: velizar_ listing specific MB misplays by unit type during a discussion about creating a ban list for bot practice.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Master Bot misplays Grimbotch by purchasing it too frequently, and misplays Rhino in unspecified ways. It also misplays Tia Threnody inconsistently: sometimes buying it against excessive defense (making it ineffective) and sometimes failing to defend against opponent Tia Threnody properly.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: velizar_
MB_WEAKNESS.context: velizar_ and masn6811 enumerating Master Bot misplays by specific unit during a bot practice ban-list discussion.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Master Bot does not use Auric Impulse correctly; however, since Auric Impulse has low strategic impact, this weakness has minimal effect on game outcomes.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: velizar_
MB_WEAKNESS.context: velizar_ and randomflyingtaco discussing which units to ban when practicing against MB, noting Auric's misplay has low impact.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Master Bot does not correctly use Resophore and does not appear to understand its strategic application.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: velizar_
MB_WEAKNESS.context: velizar_ enumerating specific unit misplays by Master Bot during a ban-list discussion for bot practice.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Master Bot misplays Vivid Drone sets: it will sometimes run out of Drones to hold and then get breached through Vivid Drone interactions.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: velizar_
MB_WEAKNESS.context: velizar_ and randomflyingtaco debating whether Vivid Drone should be on MB ban lists for practice, with velizar_ noting the specific failure mode.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Master Bot makes a specific poor opening with Doomed Drone: as P1, it plays DD turn 1, then turn 2 buys two Doomed Drones and nothing else, floating 4 gold. This has been directly observed by flakmaniak.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: flakmaniak
MB_WEAKNESS.context: flakmaniak directly observing and reporting a specific MB opening misplay with Doomed Drone.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Master Bot tends to go natural Animus even in sets where delaying would be stronger, giving the opponent extra time to develop their position safely.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: flakmaniak
MB_WEAKNESS.context: Discussion of a set with Centurion, Tia Threnody, Endotherm Kit, and Cluster Bolt where flakmaniak noted the bot went natural Animus, allowing a comfortable turn-6 Centurion purchase.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Master Bot is poor at buying large absorbers in a timely fashion, often failing to build Centurion when it is clearly indicated by the set.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: p0lari
MB_WEAKNESS.context: p0lari commented 'the bot is pretty bad at buying the big absorber' after flakmaniak described the bot failing to build Centurion while he built it on turn 6.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Master Bot over-purchases Grimbotch relative to persistent attackers like Tarsier, leaving itself without sustained attack pressure once Grimbotches die.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: flakmaniak
MB_WEAKNESS.context: flakmaniak observed the bot buys too many Grimbotches and not enough Tarsiers, and that once its Grimbotches die it loses persistent attack; community debated whether this is actually suboptimal given Grimbotch's strength.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Master Bot appears to treat a Gauss Charge as simply a cheap Gauss Cannon (i.e. +1 damage next turn) without accounting for whether the opponent has absorb that will nullify it entirely.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: zera777
MB_WEAKNESS.context: zera777 summarized the community's analysis of why the bot buys Gauss Charges into absorb: it evaluates them as cheap Gauss Cannons without modeling the opponent's defensive structure.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: The Master Bot cannot actually run out of time on its turn; its clock stays at 0 seconds indefinitely, making bot games appear much longer in playtime than intended (e.g., showing 56 minutes).
MB_WEAKNESS.author: velizar_
MB_WEAKNESS.context: velizar_ observing that the bot timer stays at 0 and never expires, inflating the displayed game duration.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: In the 'Extremely Unfair Challenge' set (Wild Drone, Auric, Cryo Ray, Aegis, Tantalum Ray, Manticore, Shredder, Polywall, Corpus, Chieftain, Feral Warden), the Master Bot's opening varies (DD/DDB, DD/D1B, etc.) and tends to rush Shredders, but players who beat the bot reported using a Wild Drone + Shredder rush rather than a defensive or high-economy build. The bot's variable openings make it harder to exploit with a single strategy.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: masn6811
MB_WEAKNESS.context: flakmaniak and masn6811 discussing how to beat the Master Bot in the Extremely Unfair Challenge scenario, with masn6811 noting the bot plays differently each time and a Wild/Shredder rush was their winning approach.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Master Bot does not resign at appropriately losing positions and may continue playing until the opponent has literal lethal on the board. A previous version of Master Bot resigned at more appropriate times but was changed after player complaints.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: masn6811
MB_WEAKNESS.context: Amalloy noted MB didn't resign on turn 13 when resignation criteria were obviously met; masn6811 confirmed this is a known and longstanding issue.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Master Bot will pursue highly specialized multi-building strategies of questionable value, such as buying triple Blastforge purely to spam Pixies, suggesting it does not adequately evaluate opportunity costs of tech overinvestment.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: velizar_
MB_WEAKNESS.context: Velizar_ reported observing Master Bot go triple Blastforge just to spam Pixies in a single observed game.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Master Bot failed to hold its Drones when the player could breach for Lucina, suggesting it has a weakness in evaluating whether breach threats are credible when none of the player's attackers were being blocked.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: velizar_
MB_WEAKNESS.context: velizar_ noted Master Bot let them breach for Lucina rather than holding Drones, and remarked the bot likely did not mis-predict the attack itself.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Master Bot can misallocate resources badly in certain sets; in one reviewed game it wasted 8 gold buying two Conduits suboptimally while facing a Doomed Wall defensive situation, leaving it vulnerable to a slow but effective attacker build.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: amalloy
MB_WEAKNESS.context: amalloy critiqued a game Zera shared as a Master Bot win, noting the bot's poor Conduit purchases were the actual cause of the human's advantage.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Master Bot went to 19 Drones against a mono-frontline (Doomed Wall) strategy, which bky flagged as a notable error (overdroning), though amalloy argued 19 drones may be fine with Doomed Wall since one Doomed Wall per turn can defend against all 4 attackers, giving plenty of time to build up counter-attack.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: consensus
MB_WEAKNESS.context: amalloy and bky debated whether Master Bot's 19-drone build against a Doomed Wall defensive opponent was correct or an AI error.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Master Bot can be beaten consistently by players who know its patterns and what it will not do, even at the 1600-1700 rating level. A player rated 1600-1700 can beat Master Bot almost every time by exploiting its predictable decision-making, despite that rating being well below top human players.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: awaclus
MB_WEAKNESS.context: Discussion about the relative skill gap between Master Bot and human ladder players of various ratings.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Master Bot does not offer draws and accepts any draw offer made by the game system whenever the draw criteria are met, rather than evaluating whether accepting is strategically correct. It is described as natural that Master Bot would resign in such situations.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: shadourow
MB_WEAKNESS.context: Discussion about draw mechanics and whether Master Bot offers or accepts draws, prompted by a game situation.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Master Bot is very weak to the Immolite rush line where P2 plays 1 Animus + Conduit on turn 1 and Gauss Cannon + Engineer on turn 2 (written as 'A .. CD / 11 .. GE'). Shadourow used this line specifically to complete a Kappa challenge against Master Bot.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: shadourow
MB_WEAKNESS.context: Discussion of strategies that exploit Master Bot's weaknesses, in the context of jeacaveo losing to an Immolite rush.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Practicing against Master Bot has limited value because players eventually learn to exploit the bot's specific misplays rather than developing general game skill. After enough exposure, improvement is against the bot's patterns rather than against human opponents.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: jeacaveo
MB_WEAKNESS.context: A new player asked whether practicing against Master Bot is worthwhile; jeacaveo gave this assessment and noted others might differ.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Master Bot plays poorly in sets featuring Galvani Drone combined with Antima Comet and Chrono Filter, giving a human player a large head start regardless of the other units in the set. Galvani Drone with Electrovore is similarly exploitable, with the bot making defensive mistakes when evaluating positions involving both units simultaneously.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: amalloy
MB_WEAKNESS.context: amalloy and bky discussed which units Master Bot handles worst, settling on combos involving Galvani Drone as the most reliable weakness.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Master Bot lacks foresight when Infusion Grid is in the set, making it exploitable by a player using Trinity Drone's Player 2 flexibility in slower games featuring Infusion Grid.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: .bky_1556
MB_WEAKNESS.context: bky outlined a preferred set for achieving a win streak against Master Bot, explaining why certain units exploit known bot weaknesses.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Master Bot is surprisingly easy to beat as P1 on base set. velizar_ reported not losing a single game as P1 against Master Bot, even when playing suboptimal openings like DD/DB.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: velizar_
MB_WEAKNESS.context: velizar_ was discussing base set P1 play against Master Bot and noted consistent wins even with suboptimal lines, with amalloy sardonically agreeing it was 'unsurprisingly easy'.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Master Bot responded to a P1 CDS opening by playing TR (Tarsier-Rhino), which velizar_ described as Master Bot 'throwing' the game, implying TR is a weak or losing response to CDS in that position.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: velizar_
MB_WEAKNESS.context: velizar_ was testing a base set opening chart against Master Bot and observed MB play a poor response to the CDS line.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Master Bot exhibits a pattern of never buying any attackers before resigning in certain sets, instead only buying absorb and soak units. This was observed in a specific replay where the bot built only defensive units and then resigned without having purchased any offensive units.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: velizar_
MB_WEAKNESS.context: velizar_ shared a replay of a bot game in the context of discussing bot behavior in the custom RPS (Rhino/Porcefield/Steelsplitter) set.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Master Bot is not very good at base-set-only games restricted to Rhino, Forcefield, and Steelsplitter (the 'RPS' variant). A strategy of buying Blastforge followed by four or five Steelsplitters (B/S/S/S/S) appears strong against it, with velizar_ winning as both P1 and P2 using this line.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: velizar_
MB_WEAKNESS.context: velizar_ was experimenting with a custom RPS (Rhino/Porcefield/Steelsplitter) restricted set against Master Bot and reported the bot struggled against a heavy Steelsplitter rush.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Master Bot is systematically poor at using chill abilities, failing to prioritize high-value chill targets such as Barriers over low-value targets such as Walls when the latter do not affect block outcomes.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: alex319
MB_WEAKNESS.context: Observation from a reported replay in which MB wasted multiple chill shots on suboptimal targets.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Master Bot can get stuck in a pattern where it never buys any attackers because it must spend all resources on defense, effectively stalling indefinitely without making offensive progress.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: velizar_
MB_WEAKNESS.context: velizar_ mentioned experiencing bot games where Master Bot was permanently locked into pure defense with no attacker purchases.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Master Bot failed to recognize that clicking Ebb allows the resources generated to be used to purchase additional Drones the same turn, missing a basic resource-chaining interaction.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: velizar_
MB_WEAKNESS.context: velizar_ shared a replay showing Master Bot missing the Ebb-into-Drone purchase optimization, describing it sarcastically as 'the bot discovers the hack'.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Certain achievement-requiring set-ups that are impossibly costly to pursue in competitive play are easy to execute against the bot, indicating the bot does not apply sufficient pressure to punish slow/greedy win-condition setups.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: consensus
MB_WEAKNESS.context: siepu, awaclus, and velizar_ discussing a rare achievement that can be set up vs the bot but would result in losing a real competitive game due to the opponent conceding or winning first.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Master Bot (MB) tends to buy drones too late into the game, well past when drone purchases are economically efficient. An Ebb Turbine strategy exploits this indirectly by removing that bad choice from the bot's decision tree.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: velizar_
MB_WEAKNESS.context: velizar_ observed that the bot keeps buying drones when it shouldn't, and noted that Ebb Turbine clicks remove that poor option.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Master Bot struggles to defend against Scorchilla rush strategies. masn6811 tested a Scorchilla-based rush against the bot, won, and re-tested several months later to find it still worked, suggesting the bot's defense against this line had not improved.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: masn6811
MB_WEAKNESS.context: masn6811 recalled repeatedly beating MB with a Scorchilla rush over multiple sessions months apart.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Master Bot is not good at planning and makes awful plays frequently, including poor openings such as D1 DDB and buying tech for advanced-set units with no coherent plan. It should not be used as a learning resource for strategic decision-making.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: amalloy
MB_WEAKNESS.context: amalloy explaining Master Bot's generally poor play after someone asked why it made an inexplicable opening, with spiritfryer agreeing learners should use GM bots instead.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Master Bot occasionally resigns from drawn or even winning positions, representing a significant evaluation error.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: amalloy
MB_WEAKNESS.context: amalloy listing known Master Bot deficiencies when discussing whether the bot should be improved to avoid resigning early.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Master Bot may play a slower economic game when Blood Pact is in the set, possibly because it sees Blood Pact as providing extra soak and therefore delays building attack pressure. This causes it to over-rely on Blood Pact for defense instead of building enough attack to prevent the opponent from accumulating a massive threat.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: awaclus
MB_WEAKNESS.context: Discussion of Master Bot's tendencies when Blood Pact is available in the set, following a list of MB's perceived strategic errors.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Master Bot exhibits a list of consistent strategic errors including: always buying Mobile Animus, treating Rhino as the best early attacker universally, buying Cluster Bolt even when the opponent has 5 attack and Polywall is present, using Blood Pact as primary defense, and spending limited Chill even when it denies 0 absorb.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: zera777
MB_WEAKNESS.context: Zera compiled a humorous but specific list of Master Bot's known strategic mistakes based on observation of its play patterns.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Top players report winning approximately 95% of games against Master Bot, with at least one top player claiming to never lose against it. Losses to Master Bot among strong players are largely attributed to playing on autopilot rather than the bot being competitive.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: amalloy
MB_WEAKNESS.context: Discussion of how strong players perform against Master Bot, with amalloy citing personal experience and mrguy888's position on losses being due to inattention.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Master Bot bought a Resophore with no Drones, meaning it would likely never generate any attack value from the unit, demonstrating it does not properly evaluate units that require Drone sacrifices as a resource condition.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: awaclus
MB_WEAKNESS.context: awaclus explained to a newer player why the bot's Resophore purchase was a mistake, noting it had no Drones to sacrifice.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Master Bot understands and executes Iso Kronus synchronization, a non-trivial tactical concept involving timing cooldown attackers to fire simultaneously for maximum effect.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: liquideggproduct
MB_WEAKNESS.context: Discussion of how bots handle cooldown attackers; liquideggproduct noted that Master Bot does sync Isos, with amalloy noting bots in general may not.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: The Q defense algorithm prioritizes soaking based on unit cost (hp/cost ratio) and does not account for stamina, causing it to irrationally deprioritize soaking 0-stamina units like Deadeye Operative and Bombarder—treating expensive but currently inactive units as high-priority to preserve.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: apooche
MB_WEAKNESS.context: Community discussion about Q defense misbehavior, specifically soaking 3 Perforators over 2 Steelsplitters.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Master Bot appears to never buy Gauss Cannon charges (also referred to as 'gauss charges') in at least some game states, making Cluster Bolt a potentially free win against it when the bot fails to respond to the threat.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: aa01blue
MB_WEAKNESS.context: aa01blue observed MB not buying gauss charges, leading to a near-win with Cluster Bolt due to supply constraints.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Master Bot does buy Cluster Bolt in specific edge cases, such as to kill Bulldogs (units with low HP), but likely avoids it when it would complicate defense logic or negatively impact defensive calculations.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: liquideggproduct
MB_WEAKNESS.context: Discussion about whether MB ever buys Cluster Bolt; liquideggproduct confirmed it buys CB to kill specific low-HP units like bulls.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Master Bot has confirmed instances of buying Cluster Bolt, as evidenced by replay codes NvCJw-NGHu4 and jVWAk-RksBn shared by liquideggproduct.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: liquideggproduct
MB_WEAKNESS.context: liquideggproduct shared replays demonstrating MB purchasing Cluster Bolt to counter the claim it never buys it.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Master Bot generally has trouble understanding that certain units (such as Immolites and Centurion) are not intended as soak targets, and will incorrectly assign damage to them during defense even when those units should be protected or used differently.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: .holyfire
MB_WEAKNESS.context: .holyfire generalized from the Tia/Centurion bug report that MB has a broader pattern of misidentifying valid soak targets.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Master Bot (7s) is notably weak against green-heavy sets; aa01blue reports rolling full green sets as P1 and winning because MB forgets to buy absorb for several turns.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: aa01blue
MB_WEAKNESS.context: Discussion about whether 7s MB is significantly stronger than 3s MB; aa01blue described exploiting MB's green set weakness.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Master Bot plays suboptimally in Zemora Voidbringer + Cluster Bolt sets, mishandling the opening and failing to compete with a human player's Zemora timing and Cluster Bolt sequencing.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: silentslayers
MB_WEAKNESS.context: After reviewing a bot game replay, silentslayers noted it was 'a bit of a non-game cause master bot botched the opening.'

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Master Bot does not know how to play Mobile Animus effectively, making sets featuring it easy to exploit against the bot.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: pikachumemes.
MB_WEAKNESS.context: Discussing a Wincer + Cryo Ray set game against Master Bot where the bot's handling of Mobile Animus was called out as a specific weakness.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Master Bot ignores unit stamina and health when assigning defense, leading to absurd choices such as soaking on a spent Bombarder via Xeno, absorbing on a spent Corpus instead of a Wall, targeting a 2-health Tantalum Ray over a Steelsplitter with Apollo, and absorbing on Borehole Patroller over Steelsplitter or Doomed Wall.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: .holyfire
MB_WEAKNESS.context: Multiple observations by .holyfire about MB's defense assignment logic ignoring stamina and health values.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Master Bot values units primarily based on their purchase cost rather than their current in-game value. This causes it to overvalue Borehole Patroller (not accounting for the Pixie cost already spent) and to absorb on more expensive units even when cheaper units are better defenders.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: apooche
MB_WEAKNESS.context: apooche explaining the general principle behind MB's repeated poor unit-valuation decisions observed by .holyfire and liquideggproduct.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Master Bot sometimes buys Mobile Animus and then double-clicks it the following turn, wasting the stamina it bought it for.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: consensus
MB_WEAKNESS.context: liquideggproduct noted MB loves Mobile Animus; siepu added it double-clicks it the next turn.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: In an observed game, Master Bot spent resources on Gauss Charges and Rhinos without apparent strategic coherence before committing to Tia Threnody, resulting in a position where the human player could comfortably reply with Protoplasm into Tia Threnody supported by Fission Turret.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: amalloy
MB_WEAKNESS.context: amalloy analyzing a game cale shared where MB made incoherent purchases before committing to Tia Threnody.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Master Bot has known weaknesses in evaluating long lead-time units like Zemora Voidbringer and Reso, using freeze abilities effectively, and making correct defensive decisions against attackers that cost energy.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: liquideggproduct
MB_WEAKNESS.context: Discussion about why Master Bot cannot achieve top-level play, with specific failure modes enumerated.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Master Bot's core limitations are: (1) a restricted set of sub-turn algorithms that prevent it from generating certain strong moves, and (2) evaluating board states by total unit cost rather than accurate positional value. Searching deeper does not improve it due to these structural limitations.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: apooche
MB_WEAKNESS.context: apooche described Master Bot's internal MCTS architecture and identified the two primary bottlenecks limiting its strength.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Master Bot does not consider blue as a viable second player strategy, according to liquideggproduct's explicit calculation; it would lose against red as P2 but not necessarily against other colors.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: liquideggproduct
MB_WEAKNESS.context: Discussion analyzing a replay where the strategic calculation was made specifically because the opponent was Master Bot and its known color-strategy limitations.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Master Bot was designed with generalized AI so it would not break with new units, which makes it less optimal than a narrower bot could be now that the game is no longer receiving new units. A tighter, more game-specific AI could outperform it.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: _wonderboat
MB_WEAKNESS.context: wonderboat explained the design philosophy behind Master Bot's generalization trade-off versus a game-specific AI.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Casual queue bots sometimes exhibit highly predictable mono-strategy behavior (e.g., opening DD/DD/CCC into Venge Cannon spam), making them easy to read and counter once the pattern is recognized. Master Bot is noted as appearing in casual queues in disguise, identifiable by longer think times.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: wontrice, swapgo, supergreyman
MB_WEAKNESS.context: New players discussing their experiences against casual queue bots and noticing predictable one-track strategies.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: The 'TotallyInsane' bot (internally named 'BL_Rush_Dumb') is programmed to not prioritize defending, making it exploitable by aggressive strategies that apply early pressure.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: kristian1103
MB_WEAKNESS.context: Discussion about bot rankings and bot behavior in casual queue, where wiwiweb revealed the internal name of the TotallyInsane bot.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Master Bot uses alpha-beta search with playouts evaluated by a simpler bot similar to Adept Bot, and its move quality can vary between the web and desktop clients because the desktop version runs native C++ code (much faster) while the web version runs ActionScript, potentially limiting how deeply the AI can search within its time limit.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: mtanzer
MB_WEAKNESS.context: Discussion about whether Master Bot is deterministic and why it sometimes makes different moves on the same position between web and desktop clients.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Master Bot is only reliably good at acquiring early blue units; sets that reward early blue tech (e.g., Ebb, Flame Animus) are cited as reasons to use Master Bot as a training opponent.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: liadahlia
MB_WEAKNESS.context: Discussion of when Master Bot is an appropriate opponent choice based on set composition.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Master Bot does not effectively exploit opponent mistakes in complex sets. In a reviewed game (iIHFt-Nc6gh), the player made numerous serious errors including wasted Frostbites, buying multiple unused Blastforges, and misusing resources, yet still won comfortably, suggesting Master Bot fails to punish suboptimal play in high-complexity sets.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: p0lari
MB_WEAKNESS.context: Replay review where a new player made major errors against Master Bot but still won easily.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Master Bot plays suboptimally in complex sets with many advanced units (b+11). A rated ~2000 player (p0lari) noted having no idea how to play a b+11 set, implying Master Bot in such sets is not a reliable teacher or strong opponent, and new players should use simpler sets (b+8) when learning.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: p0lari
MB_WEAKNESS.context: Discussion where p0lari reviewed a new player's loss against Master Bot in a b+11 set.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Master Bot failed to correctly prioritize buying Arka in a set where it was clearly the dominant unit, even after p0lari (a ~2000-rated player) noted forgetting about Arka themselves when attempting to play the set. This suggests Master Bot may miss dominant late-game unit purchases in complex sets.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: p0lari
MB_WEAKNESS.context: p0lari attempted to play the same set as the player they were coaching against Master Bot and also forgot about Arka.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Master Bot handles a Turn 2 Smorcus rush very poorly. In testing with a Galvani Drone set, the bot failed to respond adequately to the early threat, making it easy to win and claim the Rush Master achievement.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: .bky_1556
MB_WEAKNESS.context: bky discovered while testing a Smorcus/Galvani opening that Master Bot does not respond correctly to a T2 Smorcus rush.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Master Bot also fails to handle Galvani Drone variants of early rush strategies, not just the Smorcus variant. Multiple replay codes confirm the same exploitable pattern.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: .bky_1556
MB_WEAKNESS.context: After finding MB weak to T2 Smorcus, bky confirmed MB is also weak to Galvani variants of the same early rush.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Playing against the casual bot (referred to implicitly) is not strong evidence of a line being correct. amalloy notes that beating the casual bot with an early Animus line did not validate it as a good strategic choice against strong human play.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: amalloy
MB_WEAKNESS.context: amalloy questioning whether their P1 Animus line was good, noting it beat the casual bot but that this was not strong evidence.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Master Bot that rushes Apollo very hard is forced heavily into defense once the opponent develops even a single efficient attacker (e.g., Xeno), limiting the bot to around five attack and making it unable to generate meaningful pressure with Apollo beyond clicking Conduits or Forges.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: p0lari
MB_WEAKNESS.context: Expert analyzing a replay against Master Bot to find how to counter its Apollo rush.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Master Bot plays Rhinos poorly in sets where Electrovore rush is the correct strategy. In a set containing Electrovore, Master Bot bought Rhinos when the dominant line is considered to be a fast Electrovore opener.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: nvlx
MB_WEAKNESS.context: User uchinanchu shared a game vs Master Bot; nvlx (Fate) immediately noted the bot's Rhino purchases were wrong given an Electrovore-viable set.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Master Bot tends to overbuild drones, often purchasing significantly more than optimal; human players can exploit this by stopping at a lower drone count and transitioning to attack earlier.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: amalloy
MB_WEAKNESS.context: amalloy shared a game where he stopped at 11 drones while his human opponent went to 16, noting that Master Bot commonly makes the same over-droning mistake.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Master Bot frequently buys Centrifuge but fails to do anything relevant with it, and when it buys Zemora Voidbringer it often never fires it, making these units reliable ways to exploit the bot's inability to use complex conditional units.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: nvlx
MB_WEAKNESS.context: Discussion about how to beat Master Bot for achievements; nvlx and f300xen noted the bot's systematic misuse of Centrifuge and Zemora.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Master Bot frequently buys multiple mobile Animus units but loses because it fails to efficiently use the red resources they generate, suggesting the bot cannot properly plan around click-based red economy units.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: shadourow
MB_WEAKNESS.context: shadourow and nvlx observing Master Bot's tendency to buy many mobile Animus and Rhinos without effectively spending the red resources.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Master Bot's Defense Grid rush in the referenced game was not the correct sequence, indicating it does not execute the optimal Defense Grid rush line.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: masn6811
MB_WEAKNESS.context: masn6811 noted that Master Bot's Defense Grid rush was suboptimal and provided the correct defensive line.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Master Bot applies very little pressure against any strategy, making it poor practice for human players. It commonly opens with weak lines like DD DD DDA DDB, and does not adapt its responses to opponent strategies — for example, it may buy Walls and Tarsiers defensively even when the human opponent has a Savior, making the human's builds appear wrong when they are actually fine against a real opponent.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: masn6811
MB_WEAKNESS.context: masn6811 and amalloy discussed why playing against Master Bot is not good practice, giving specific examples of its passive and non-adaptive play.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Master Bot's defensive choices are highly predictable — for example, it will sacrifice a Rhino instead of a Wall when given the choice, allowing human players to gambit their Centurion with confidence. This predictability would not be exploitable against a human opponent.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: masn6811
MB_WEAKNESS.context: masn6811 gave the Rhino-vs-Wall sacrifice as a concrete example of Master Bot's predictable defensive decisions.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Master Bot makes no effort to protect its Borehole Patrollers, making absorb-denial strategies appear less useful than they actually are in real games — players may underutilize absorb denial because the bot never contests it.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: masn6811
MB_WEAKNESS.context: masn6811 noted that unlike flame animus + absorb denial setups, vigilant-unit absorb denial strategies feel useless against Master Bot because it never protects its Boreholes.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: The Master Bot is capable of executing the Iso Kronus + Thorium Dynamo + Gauss Cannon strategy effectively enough to beat experienced players, suggesting this is a particularly strong bot strategy rather than a weakness. Multiple players reported losing to this combination when played by the bot.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: velizar_
MB_WEAKNESS.context: velizar_ reported losing multiple times to the Master Bot using Iso Kronus + Thorium Dynamo + Gauss Cannon, with a second player also reporting losses to this strategy.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: The Master Bot tends to ignore most advanced set units in complex sets, often only using a small subset of the available units (e.g., only 3 of many extra units in one observed game). Despite this, it can still win against intermediate players.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: velizar_
MB_WEAKNESS.context: velizar_ observed that the Master Bot ignored most extra set units in a game but still won, suggesting the bot simplifies its strategy by focusing on a narrow subset of units.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: The bot in the referenced game failed to respond optimally to a Cynestra-heavy line; shadourow noted that a smarter bot would have used Doomed Mech or Tesla with Cryo Ray support to obliterate the player's strategy, implying Master Bot (or the bot in question) misses these defensive counter-build opportunities.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: shadourow
MB_WEAKNESS.context: shadourow reviewed a new player's bot game and pointed out that a smarter bot would have countered with Doomed Mech or Tesla+Cryo Ray.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Master Bot plays poorly with Antima Comet, frequently misusing or wasting red production from the unit. Players have observed this in multiple games and consider it a known exploitable weakness of the bot.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: flakmaniak
MB_WEAKNESS.context: flakmaniak noted the bot seemed terrible at using Antima Comet after playing a game against Master Bot involving the unit.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Master Bot struggles with units that require planning or forethought, particularly alternate-resource-cost units and units with additional activation costs. This is a general structural weakness of the bot beyond specific unit misplay.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: iminabearsuit
MB_WEAKNESS.context: Discussion of why Master Bot misplays Arka and Doomed Mech combinations, with iminabearsuit generalizing this to all units requiring multi-turn planning.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Master Bot is described as frightening to newer players but becomes increasingly easy to exploit as a player gains experience, implying its strength ceiling is well below high-level human play.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: shadourow
MB_WEAKNESS.context: shadourow commented on Master Bot's difficulty curve while discussing a replay where flakmaniak easily beat it with a Doomed Drone opening.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Master Bot plays poorly against Centurion; buying Centurion is considered the stronger play even when Vai Mauronax is in the set, because the bot cannot effectively exploit Vai or counter Centurion, making it an easily exploitable weakness.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: jeacaveo
MB_WEAKNESS.context: jeacaveo reported that testing against Master Bot confirmed buying Centurion was correct even with Vai in the set, implying the bot cannot leverage Vai to counter Centurion effectively.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Master Bot will sometimes pursue a natural Animus in sets where it is questionable (e.g., sets with lots of defense and no obvious red synergy), floating gold with no clear follow-up plan on turn 4.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: flakmaniak
MB_WEAKNESS.context: flakmaniak noted Master Bot went DD/DD/A11, floating two gold, in a set with Zemora and heavy defense; jeacaveo agreed Master Bot is often suboptimal.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: The bot playing a 'drivenupthewall' (mono-blue Wall-stacking) strategy refused to consider green units, causing it to run out of Wall supply and lose to a player who otherwise got badly breached.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: .bky_1556
MB_WEAKNESS.context: bky explaining why matrix30815 won despite being breached: the bot exhausted its Wall supply by ignoring green options.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Master Bot sometimes buys drones late into the game when it should instead be buying attackers or other units. This pattern of over-investing in economy past the optimal point is a recurring weakness observed across multiple games.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: velizar_
MB_WEAKNESS.context: masn6811 shared a replay where Master Bot misplayed the endgame, prompting velizar_ and masn6811 to discuss the bot's general tendency to buy drones too late.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Master Bot plays poorly against Antima Comet; a player can win games they would otherwise lose because the bot mishandles the Antima Comet threat (e.g., using it for only 1 damage when it could deal 10).
MB_WEAKNESS.author: amalloy
MB_WEAKNESS.context: amalloy analyzing a new player's replay against Master Bot and noting the player only won due to the bot's poor Antima Comet play.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Master Bot appears to rarely or never buy Cluster Bolt even in situations where buying 4 Cluster Bolts would be a winning move, preferring Gauss Charge instead. Lyra observed the bot hoarding green resources without spending them on Cluster Bolt, resulting in a draw rather than a win for the bot.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: lyra1712
MB_WEAKNESS.context: Discussion of bot behavior while attempting achievements involving Master Bot in custom sets with Venge Cannon, Galvani Drone, and Cluster Bolt.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Master Bot can be beaten using a Zemora Voidbringer line in a Bloodrager + Polywall set, though the matchup is close enough that it is likely not viable against a human opponent without excellent play.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: awaclus
MB_WEAKNESS.context: Post-replay analysis testing Zemora lines against Master Bot in a Bloodrager set.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: The P2 DD/DD/DDAA/Tatsu Nullifier opening worked against Master Bot but is considered easily beatable by expert players (e.g. with Gauss Cannon spam), suggesting Master Bot does not correctly punish this greedy early line.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: awaclus
MB_WEAKNESS.context: A player asking if a Tatsu Nullifier opening is legitimate after it worked against Master Bot; experts indicate it should lose to correct play.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Master Bot opened natural Blastforge 'for no reason at all' in a base+1 set where Animo (Amporilla) was available, which pikachumemes considered a clear mistake. This suggests MB makes suboptimal opening decisions in sets where the correct response to an opponent's opening is not straightforward.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: pikachumemes.
MB_WEAKNESS.context: holyfire shared a game replay and pikachumemes analyzed it, noting MB's inexplicable natural Blastforge opening.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: In the 'Extremely Unfair' challenge scenario, Master Bot sometimes stops absorbing entirely despite having defense available, apparently because it detects frontline units (Wild Drones) and decides absorbing is suboptimal. This causes it to give up a +2 absorb-per-turn advantage to the player, and it can be exploited by achieving tempo before the bot switches to its Polywall-buying routine.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: spiritfryer
MB_WEAKNESS.context: spiritfryer writing a detailed walkthrough for the 'Extremely Unfair' bot challenge, discovering the AI stops absorbing due to frontline unit logic.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Master Bot is considered very weak as an opponent; beating it with any strategy, including aggressive openings like Base + Smorcus, is not considered meaningful evidence of a unit's power level. Community consensus is that MB losses carry no analytical weight.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: consensus
MB_WEAKNESS.context: A player claimed Smorcus was OP after beating Master Bot with it; experts dismissed this as meaningless.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Master Bot plays poorly with Zemora Voidbringer and Mobile Animus. Even with units it handles better (e.g., Thunderhead), it exhibits specific misplays: it prioritizes building Wall over setting up 7 damage, and absorbs on Wall instead of the Rhino that would provide the 7th point of damage needed for a key threshold.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: masn6811
MB_WEAKNESS.context: Discussion about which units Master Bot plays well or poorly with, prompted by velizar's suggestion to document bot-friendly sets.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Master Bot is noted to be reasonably good at red rushing strategies, making it harder to beat with aggressive red-based openings compared to other approaches.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: totally_not_fbi
MB_WEAKNESS.context: A player mentioned struggling to beat Master Bot after many attempts, and totally_not_fbi commented that Master Bot is kinda good at red rushing.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: A player reports consistently being able to beat Master Bot as Player 2 but finding it very difficult to beat as Player 1, suggesting Master Bot may have an exploitable asymmetry or that P1 play against it is significantly harder.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: kaom.
MB_WEAKNESS.context: kaom asking for resources on beating Master Bot as P1 after consistently beating it as P2.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Master Bot is described as 'pretty basic,' with community members implying it plays predictably and can be beaten with straightforward strategies. The specific question of whether Master Bot used Tia Threnody on itself (a self-sacrifice play) suggests it may misuse or trigger Tia Threnody incorrectly.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: amalloy
MB_WEAKNESS.context: Brief exchange about Master Bot's play quality and whether it made a Tia Threnody misplay.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Master Bot performs poorly in sets containing Lucina (likely a misspelling or nickname for a unit), tending to buy quadruple Tarsier over more situationally appropriate units like Lucina, suggesting it does not properly evaluate conditional or complex units.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: masn6811
MB_WEAKNESS.context: masn6811 commented that Master Bot is bad with 'say lucina' and prefers quad Tarsier over it, implying a known bot evaluation weakness in specific sets.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Scorchilla and Iso Kronus are perceived as easy ways to beat Master Bot, suggesting the bot has difficulty handling fast or unconventional pressure from these units.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: simmarine
MB_WEAKNESS.context: simmarine reflected on bot games, noting these units tend to produce easy wins against Master Bot.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Master Bot reportedly used to buy out the entire Arms Race supply before the human player had purchased Antima Comet, effectively denying the player the ability to build a large Antima. Namington noted this behavior may have been patched out.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: namington
MB_WEAKNESS.context: Discussion of bot behavior after undefeatedorca mentioned an 86-damage Antima used against a bot.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: crash_overlord reports having beaten Master Bot in a bullet blindfolded game, suggesting Master Bot has exploitable weaknesses even under highly constrained player conditions.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: crash_overlord
MB_WEAKNESS.context: crash_overlord mentioned beating Master Bot blindfolded in bullet as a notable accomplishment, similar to mrguy888's documented blindfolded games.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Master Bot appears to evaluate Corpus as comparable in value to Wall based on raw gold-equivalent cost (Corpus costs 6RR ≈ 8 gold; Wall costs 5B ≈ 6.67 gold), and may prefer Corpus over Wall in situations where Wall is the strategically correct defensive buy. This suggests MB has weaknesses in evaluating defensive unit selection beyond raw gold cost.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: shadourow
MB_WEAKNESS.context: Shadourow comments on a bot decision to buy Corpus over Wall, sarcastically noting the bot's apparent gold-equivalence logic.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Master Bot can be exploited by obtaining strong unit combinations like Vai and Amporilla too easily, suggesting it does not adequately contest or react to the opponent setting up powerful synergistic unit pairs.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: flakmaniak
MB_WEAKNESS.context: flakmaniak noted the bot 'probably shouldn't have let' them acquire Vai and Amporilla so easily in a set with Vivid and Doomed Drone.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Master Bot sometimes does not concede in clearly lost positions (e.g. one turn from having all units destroyed), but will concede if the opponent passes their attack phase without attacking.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: velizar_
MB_WEAKNESS.context: velizar_ described a situation where the bot failed to concede a hopeless position until the opponent skipped their attack, suggesting the bot's resignation logic has edge cases.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Master Bot is also weak to an Omega high-economy strategy using Diffusion Grid with maximum soak and no attackers. Shadourow lists this alongside the Immolite line as a reliable way to beat the bot.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: shadourow
MB_WEAKNESS.context: Shadourow listing multiple strategies that reliably defeat Master Bot, including high-econ no-attacker lines.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: The Master Bot employs a 'freeze you to death' strategy using a specific unit or combination, implying it predictably commits to a freezing/tempo-locking line that human players can anticipate and counter.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: velizar_
MB_WEAKNESS.context: velizar_ briefly noted the bot uses a 'freeze you to death' strategy in reference to an unspecified game or set being discussed.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Master Bot is not a strong defender and can be exploited by strategies that would be less reliable against human players. aa01blue notes that all their Master Bot losses involve fastimus Tarsier lines when attempting 'fancypants' strategies, suggesting MB punishes greedy play but struggles to defend against focused pressure.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: aa01blue
MB_WEAKNESS.context: Discussion about Master Bot difficulty in the context of base set strategy for new players.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Tactics-heavy positions (e.g., Elyot Animus lines) are described as the bot's strongest point, implying Master Bot handles tactical exchanges better than strategic/economic planning in complex sets.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: namington
MB_WEAKNESS.context: Discussion of whether Elyot Animus was viable for a new player against bot opponents; namington noted tactics are where bots are strongest.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Master Bot struggles against Lucina in sets where a Fuge (Fuguede?) is available — a slower Fuge line was tested and lost to Master Bot's Tarsiers and Polywalls, suggesting MB plays Tarsier/Polywall aggressively in those sets. This was noted as an anecdotal observation, not a systematic exploit.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: amalloy
MB_WEAKNESS.context: amalloy mentions testing a slower Fuge line against Master Bot and losing to its Tarsier/Polywall play.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Master Bot does weird things with Mobile Animus, suggesting it does not evaluate the unit correctly or mishandles multi-Animus board states.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: ggmoyang
MB_WEAKNESS.context: ggmoyang briefly mentioned unusual Master Bot behavior with Mobile Animus; amalloy noted that owning two Mobile Animuses is rare but can be correct.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: The Master Bot sometimes plays a Blastforge opening in sets where it is suboptimal, producing a noticeably weaker strategy than its usual lines. This was observed when a player replayed the same set against the bot and it switched to a Blastforge opening.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: velizar_
MB_WEAKNESS.context: velizar_ noted that when replaying a set against the Master Bot, it played a Blastforge opening which was described as much worse than its previous strategy in that set.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: The Master Bot is capable of executing the temporarily breach-proof Thorium Dynamo strategy (going breach-proof while incoming damage is low, then switching back to defending), well enough to use it against human players successfully.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: velizar_
MB_WEAKNESS.context: velizar_ mentioned dying to the bot using the temporary Dynamo breach-proof strategy when stacko asked about its viability.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Master Bot's Cynestra play is noted by velizar_ to be beatable, attributed partly to Cynestra's GGGR cost being more inconvenient than GGGB (Plasmafier's cost), suggesting MB may not optimally handle the resource requirements of Cynestra.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: velizar_
MB_WEAKNESS.context: velizar_ half-jokingly acknowledged losing to Master Bot when going Cynestra, which sparked a discussion about cost inconvenience.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: A new player reported losing to Master Bot, suggesting MB is strong enough to defeat inexperienced players, but the community implied the player should first aim to beat Expert Bot as a baseline skill benchmark before attempting Master Bot.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: valgrim_the_demon
MB_WEAKNESS.context: valgrim_the_demon mentioned losing to Master Bot while f300xen asked if they had beaten Expert Bot, implying a skill progression ladder among bots.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: The Master Bot (and bots generally) perform poorly on custom base sets, described simply as 'trash at all custom sets' by a player who tested a modified base set removing Blastforge, Steelsplitter, Wall, Tarsier, and Gauss Cannon.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: hyreon
MB_WEAKNESS.context: Player testing a custom 'Reso War' alternate base set and noting bot quality degrades on non-standard sets.

MB_WEAKNESS.insight: Energy Matrix is identified as a unit that makes the Master Bot easy to beat when it appears in the set, suggesting the bot handles Energy Matrix sets poorly.
MB_WEAKNESS.author: velizar_
MB_WEAKNESS.context: Casual mention during an unrelated discussion thread, with velizar_ naming Energy Matrix as a unit that trivializes beating the bot.

bot_related_insights.insight: liadahlia recommends that players be able to beat Master Bot consistently before transitioning to ranked PvP, treating consistent MB wins as a benchmark of readiness for competitive play.
bot_related_insights.author: liadahlia
bot_related_insights.context: Discussion about the recommended progression path from campaign/combat training to ranked PvP for new players.

bot_related_insights.insight: A Smorcus with a pay-gold-for-attack click ability can create game states reachable only via undo (e.g., clicking Drone, Smorcus, Auride Core, then undoing Smorcus reaches a resource state the AI cannot reach and a human cannot reach without undo), which is a design concern elyot has historically avoided by not printing such abilities.
bot_related_insights.author: elyot
bot_related_insights.context: elyot explaining a specific mechanical problem with the proposed Smorcus rework that includes paying gold for attack.
